search_text: A Brain-Computer Interface Data Persistence System for Multi-Scenario and Multi-Modal Data: NeuroStore 多场景、多模态数据的脑机接口数据持久化系统：NeuroStore With the rapid advancement of brain-computer interface (BCI) technology, the volume of physiological data generated in related research and applications has grown significantly. Data is a critical resource in BCI research and a key factor in the development of BCI technology, making efficient storage and management of this data increasingly vital. In the realm of research, ample data can facilitate the development of novel algorithms, which can be more accurately validated. In terms of applications, well-organized data can foster the emergence of new business opportunities, thereby maximizing the commercial value of the data. Currently, there are two major challenges in the storage and management of BCI data: providing different classification storage modes for multi-modal data, and adapting to varying application scenarios while improving storage strategies. To address these challenges, this study has developed the NeuroStore BCI data persistence system, which provides a general and easily scalable data model and can effectively handle multiple types of data storage. The system has a flexible distributed framework and can be widely applied to various scenarios. It has been utilized as the core support platform for efficient data storage and management services in the "BCI Controlled Robot Contest in World Robot Contest." 随着脑机接口（BCI）技术的快速进步，相关研究和应用中产生的生理数据量显着增长。数据是 BCI 研究的关键资源，也是 BCI 技术发展的关键因素，因此数据的高效存储和管理变得越来越重要。在研究领域，充足的数据可以促进新算法的开发，从而可以更准确地进行验证。在应用方面，组织良好的数据可以催生新的商业机会，从而最大化数据的商业价值。目前，BCI数据的存储和管理面临两大挑战：为多模态数据提供不同的分类存储模式，并在改进存储策略的同时适应不同的应用场景。为了应对这些挑战，本研究开发了NeuroStore BCI数据持久化系统，该系统提供了通用且易于扩展的数据模型，可以有效处理多种类型的数据存储。系统具有灵活的分布式架构，可广泛应用于各种场景。已在“世界机器人大赛BCI控制机器人大赛”中作为高效数据存储和管理服务的核心支撑平台。 本文提出了一种名为NeuroStore的脑机接口（BCI）数据持久化系统，旨在解决多场景和多模态BCI数据存储与管理的挑战。 Yang Chen,Hongxin Zhang,Guanyu Xiong,Chenxu Li,Chengcheng Hong,Chen Yang cs.DB,cs.ET

authors_text: Javier Tirado-Garín,Javier Civera
title: AnyCalib: On-Manifold Learning for Model-Agnostic Single-View Camera Calibration
title_zh: AnyCalib：用于与模型无关的单视图相机校准的流形学习
abstract: We present AnyCalib, a method for calibrating the intrinsic parameters of a camera from a single in-the-wild image, that is agnostic to the camera model. Current methods are predominantly tailored to specific camera models and/or require extrinsic cues, such as the direction of gravity, to be visible in the image. In contrast, we argue that the perspective and distortion cues inherent in images are sufficient for model-agnostic camera calibration. To demonstrate this, we frame the calibration process as the regression of the rays corresponding to each pixel. We show, for the first time, that this intermediate representation allows for a closed-form recovery of the intrinsics for a wide range of camera models, including but not limited to: pinhole, Brown-Conrady and Kannala-Brandt. Our approach also applies to edited -- cropped and stretched -- images. Experimentally, we demonstrate that AnyCalib consistently outperforms alternative methods, including 3D foundation models, despite being trained on orders of magnitude less data. Code is available at https://github.com/javrtg/AnyCalib.
summary: We present AnyCalib, a method for calibrating the intrinsic parameters of a camera from a single in-the-wild image, that is agnostic to the camera model. Current methods are predominantly tailored to specific camera models and/or require extrinsic cues, such as the direction of gravity, to be visible in the image. In contrast, we argue that the perspective and distortion cues inherent in images are sufficient for model-agnostic camera calibration. To demonstrate this, we frame the calibration process as the regression of the rays corresponding to each pixel. We show, for the first time, that this intermediate representation allows for a closed-form recovery of the intrinsics for a wide range of camera models, including but not limited to: pinhole, Brown-Conrady and Kannala-Brandt. Our approach also applies to edited -- cropped and stretched -- images. Experimentally, we demonstrate that AnyCalib consistently outperforms alternative methods, including 3D foundation models, despite being trained on orders of magnitude less data. Code is available at https://github.com/javrtg/AnyCalib.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 AnyCalib，一种从单个野外图像校准相机内在参数的方法，该方法与相机模型无关。当前的方法主要是针对特定相机型号定制的和/或需要外部线索（例如重力方向）才能在图像中可见。 In contrast, we argue that the perspective and distortion cues inherent in images are sufficient for model-agnostic camera calibration. To demonstrate this, we frame the calibration process as the regression of the rays corresponding to each pixel.我们首次证明，这种中间表示允许对各种相机模型的本征进行封闭式恢复，包括但不限于：针孔、Brown-Conrady 和 Kannala-Brandt。 Our approach also applies to edited -- cropped and stretched -- images.通过实验，我们证明 AnyCalib 始终优于其他方法，包括 3D 基础模型，尽管是在少几个数量级的数据上进行训练的。 Code is available at https://github.com/javrtg/AnyCalib.

AI.tldr: 提出一种无需特定相机模型、仅通过单张图像即可校准相机内部参数的方法。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖特定模型或外部线索，而图像本身包含足够的信息进行模型无关的标定。
AI.method: 通过回归像素对应的光线，利用闭式解恢复多种相机模型的内部参数，适用于编辑后的图像。
AI.result: 实验表明，AnyCalib在多种模型上表现优于现有方法，包括3D基础模型，且训练数据量更少。
AI.conclusion: AnyCalib提供了一种通用、高效的单图像相机标定方法，适用于多种模型和编辑后的图像。

authors_json: et al. (9 additional authors not shown)
authors_text: Dazhou Guo,Zhanghexuan Ji,Yanzhou Su,Dandan Zheng,Heng Guo,Puyang Wang,Ke Yan,Yirui Wang,Qinji Yu,Zi Li,Minfeng Xu,Jianfeng Zhang,Haoshen Li,Jia Ge,Tsung-Ying Ho,Bing-Shen Huang,Tashan Ai,Kuaile Zhao,Na Shen,Qifeng Wang,Yun Bian,Tingyu Wu,Peng Du,Hua Zhang,Feng-Ming Kong, et al. (9 additional authors not shown)
title: A Continual Learning-driven Model for Accurate and Generalizable Segmentation of Clinically Comprehensive and Fine-grained Whole-body Anatomies in CT
title_zh: 一种持续学习驱动的模型，用于对 CT 中的临床综合和细粒度全身解剖进行准确和可推广的分割
abstract: Precision medicine in the quantitative management of chronic diseases and oncology would be greatly improved if the Computed Tomography (CT) scan of any patient could be segmented, parsed and analyzed in a precise and detailed way. However, there is no such fully annotated CT dataset with all anatomies delineated for training because of the exceptionally high manual cost, the need for specialized clinical expertise, and the time required to finish the task. To this end, we proposed a novel continual learning-driven CT model that can segment complete anatomies presented using dozens of previously partially labeled datasets, dynamically expanding its capacity to segment new ones without compromising previously learned organ knowledge. Existing multi-dataset approaches are not able to dynamically segment new anatomies without catastrophic forgetting and would encounter optimization difficulty or infeasibility when segmenting hundreds of anatomies across the whole range of body regions. Our single unified CT segmentation model, CL-Net, can highly accurately segment a clinically comprehensive set of 235 fine-grained whole-body anatomies. Composed of a universal encoder, multiple optimized and pruned decoders, CL-Net is developed using 13,952 CT scans from 20 public and 16 private high-quality partially labeled CT datasets of various vendors, different contrast phases, and pathologies. Extensive evaluation demonstrates that CL-Net consistently outperforms the upper limit of an ensemble of 36 specialist nnUNets trained per dataset with the complexity of 5% model size and significantly surpasses the segmentation accuracy of recent leading Segment Anything-style medical image foundation models by large margins. Our continual learning-driven CL-Net model would lay a solid foundation to facilitate many downstream tasks of oncology and chronic diseases using the most widely adopted CT imaging.
summary: Precision medicine in the quantitative management of chronic diseases and oncology would be greatly improved if the Computed Tomography (CT) scan of any patient could be segmented, parsed and analyzed in a precise and detailed way. However, there is no such fully annotated CT dataset with all anatomies delineated for training because of the exceptionally high manual cost, the need for specialized clinical expertise, and the time required to finish the task. To this end, we proposed a novel continual learning-driven CT model that can segment complete anatomies presented using dozens of previously partially labeled datasets, dynamically expanding its capacity to segment new ones without compromising previously learned organ knowledge. Existing multi-dataset approaches are not able to dynamically segment new anatomies without catastrophic forgetting and would encounter optimization difficulty or infeasibility when segmenting hundreds of anatomies across the whole range of body regions. Our single unified CT segmentation model, CL-Net, can highly accurately segment a clinically comprehensive set of 235 fine-grained whole-body anatomies. Composed of a universal encoder, multiple optimized and pruned decoders, CL-Net is developed using 13,952 CT scans from 20 public and 16 private high-quality partially labeled CT datasets of various vendors, different contrast phases, and pathologies. Extensive evaluation demonstrates that CL-Net consistently outperforms the upper limit of an ensemble of 36 specialist nnUNets trained per dataset with the complexity of 5% model size and significantly surpasses the segmentation accuracy of recent leading Segment Anything-style medical image foundation models by large margins. Our continual learning-driven CL-Net model would lay a solid foundation to facilitate many downstream tasks of oncology and chronic diseases using the most widely adopted CT imaging.
abstract_zh: 如果能够对任何患者的计算机断层扫描（CT）扫描进行精确、详细的分割、解析和分析，那么慢性病和肿瘤学定量管理中的精准医学将得到极大的提高。然而，由于手动成本极高、需要专门的临床专业知识以及完成任务所需的时间，因此没有这样一个带有完整注释的 CT 数据集，其中包含用于训练的所有解剖结构。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的持续学习驱动的 CT 模型，该模型可以分割使用数十个先前部分标记的数据集呈现的完整解剖结构，动态扩展其分割新解剖结构的能力，而不会影响先前学习的器官知识。现有的多数据集方法无法在不发生灾难性遗忘的情况下动态分割新的解剖结构，并且在分割整个身体区域范围内的数百个解剖结构时会遇到优化困难或不可行。我们的单一统一 CT 分割模型 CL-Net 可以高度准确地分割临床综合的 235 个细粒度全身解剖结构。 CL-Net 由通用编码器、多个优化和修剪的解码器组成，使用来自不同供应商、不同对比阶段和病理的 20 个公共和 16 个私有高质量部分标记 CT 数据集的 13,952 个 CT 扫描来开发。广泛的评估表明，CL-Net 始终优于每个数据集训练的 36 个专业 nnUNet 集合的上限，模型大小的复杂性为 5%，并且大大超过了最近领先的 Segment Anything 式医学图像基础模型的分割精度。我们持续学习驱动的 CL-Net 模型将为使用最广泛采用的 CT 成像促进肿瘤学和慢性病的许多下游任务奠定坚实的基础。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于持续学习的CT分割模型CL-Net，能够准确分割235种全身细粒度解剖结构。
AI.motivation: 现有方法无法动态扩展新解剖结构分割且易遗忘已学知识，全面标注的CT数据集因标注成本高而稀缺。
AI.method: 设计包含通用编码器和优化解码器的CL-Net，利用13,952例多来源CT数据进行持续学习训练。
AI.result: CL-Net在235种解剖结构分割上显著优于36个专家nnUNet模型和最新医学基础模型。
AI.conclusion: 该模型为肿瘤和慢性病的定量管理提供了可扩展的CT影像分析基础。

Composed of a universal encoder, multiple optimized and pruned decoders, CL-Net is developed using 13,952 CT scans from 20 public and 16 private high-quality partially labeled CT datasets of various vendors, different contrast phases, and pathologies. Our continual learning-driven CL-Net model would lay a solid foundation to facilitate many downstream tasks of oncology and chronic diseases using the most widely adopted CT imaging. 如果能够对任何患者的计算机断层扫描（CT）扫描进行精确、详细的分割、解析和分析，那么慢性病和肿瘤学定量管理中的精准医学将得到极大的提高。然而，由于手动成本极高、需要专门的临床专业知识以及完成任务所需的时间，因此没有这样一个带有完整注释的 CT 数据集，其中包含用于训练的所有解剖结构。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的持续学习驱动的 CT 模型，该模型可以分割使用数十个先前部分标记的数据集呈现的完整解剖结构，动态扩展其分割新解剖结构的能力，而不会影响先前学习的器官知识。现有的多数据集方法无法在不发生灾难性遗忘的情况下动态分割新的解剖结构，并且在分割整个身体区域范围内的数百个解剖结构时会遇到优化困难或不可行。我们的单一统一 CT 分割模型 CL-Net 可以高度准确地分割临床综合的 235 个细粒度全身解剖结构。 CL-Net 由通用编码器、多个优化和修剪的解码器组成，使用来自不同供应商、不同对比阶段和病理的 20 个公共和 16 个私有高质量部分标记 CT 数据集的 13,952 个 CT 扫描来开发。广泛的评估表明，CL-Net 始终优于每个数据集训练的 36 个专业 nnUNet 集合的上限，模型大小的复杂性为 5%，并且大大超过了最近领先的 Segment Anything 式医学图像基础模型的分割精度。我们持续学习驱动的 CL-Net 模型将为使用最广泛采用的 CT 成像促进肿瘤学和慢性病的许多下游任务奠定坚实的基础。 本文提出一种基于持续学习的CT分割模型CL-Net，能够准确分割235种全身细粒度解剖结构。 Dazhou Guo,Zhanghexuan Ji,Yanzhou Su,Dandan Zheng,Heng Guo,Puyang Wang,Ke Yan,Yirui Wang,Qinji Yu,Zi Li,Minfeng Xu,Jianfeng Zhang,Haoshen Li,Jia Ge,Tsung-Ying Ho,Bing-Shen Huang,Tashan Ai,Kuaile Zhao,Na Shen,Qifeng Wang,Yun Bian,Tingyu Wu,Peng Du,Hua Zhang,Feng-Ming Kong, et al. (9 additional authors not shown) eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Yuansheng Lian,Ke Zhang,Meng Li
title: CDKFormer: Contextual Deviation Knowledge-Based Transformer for Long-Tail Trajectory Prediction
title_zh: CDKFormer：用于长尾轨迹预测的基于上下文偏差知识的变压器
abstract: Predicting the future movements of surrounding vehicles is essential for ensuring the safe operation and efficient navigation of autonomous vehicles (AVs) in urban traffic environments. Existing vehicle trajectory prediction methods primarily focus on improving overall performance, yet they struggle to address long-tail scenarios effectively. This limitation often leads to poor predictions in rare cases, significantly increasing the risk of safety incidents. Taking Argoverse 2 motion forecasting dataset as an example, we first investigate the long-tail characteristics in trajectory samples from two perspectives, individual motion and group interaction, and deriving deviation features to distinguish abnormal from regular scenarios. On this basis, we propose CDKFormer, a Contextual Deviation Knowledge-based Transformer model for long-tail trajectory prediction. CDKFormer integrates an attention-based scene context fusion module to encode spatiotemporal interaction and road topology. An additional deviation feature fusion module is proposed to capture the dynamic deviations in the target vehicle status. We further introduce a dual query-based decoder, supported by a multi-stream decoder block, to sequentially decode heterogeneous scene deviation features and generate multimodal trajectory predictions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CDKFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly enhancing prediction accuracy and robustness for long-tailed trajectories compared to existing methods, thus advancing the reliability of AVs in complex real-world environments.
summary: Predicting the future movements of surrounding vehicles is essential for ensuring the safe operation and efficient navigation of autonomous vehicles (AVs) in urban traffic environments. Existing vehicle trajectory prediction methods primarily focus on improving overall performance, yet they struggle to address long-tail scenarios effectively. This limitation often leads to poor predictions in rare cases, significantly increasing the risk of safety incidents. Taking Argoverse 2 motion forecasting dataset as an example, we first investigate the long-tail characteristics in trajectory samples from two perspectives, individual motion and group interaction, and deriving deviation features to distinguish abnormal from regular scenarios. On this basis, we propose CDKFormer, a Contextual Deviation Knowledge-based Transformer model for long-tail trajectory prediction. CDKFormer integrates an attention-based scene context fusion module to encode spatiotemporal interaction and road topology. An additional deviation feature fusion module is proposed to capture the dynamic deviations in the target vehicle status. We further introduce a dual query-based decoder, supported by a multi-stream decoder block, to sequentially decode heterogeneous scene deviation features and generate multimodal trajectory predictions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CDKFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly enhancing prediction accuracy and robustness for long-tailed trajectories compared to existing methods, thus advancing the reliability of AVs in complex real-world environments.
abstract_zh: 预测周围车辆的未来运动对于确保自动驾驶车辆（AV）在城市交通环境中的安全运行和高效导航至关重要。现有的车辆轨迹预测方法主要侧重于提高整体性能，但难以有效解决长尾场景。 This limitation often leads to poor predictions in rare cases, significantly increasing the risk of safety incidents.以Argoverse 2运动预测数据集为例，我们首先从个体运动和群体交互两个角度研究轨迹样本的长尾特征，并推导偏差特征以区分异常场景和常规场景。 On this basis, we propose CDKFormer, a Contextual Deviation Knowledge-based Transformer model for long-tail trajectory prediction. CDKFormer integrates an attention-based scene context fusion module to encode spatiotemporal interaction and road topology. An additional deviation feature fusion module is proposed to capture the dynamic deviations in the target vehicle status.我们进一步引入了基于双查询的解码器，由多流解码器块支持，以顺序解码异构场景偏差特征并生成多模态轨迹预测。大量实验表明，CDKFormer 实现了最先进的性能，与现有方法相比，显着提高了长尾轨迹的预测准确性和鲁棒性，从而提高了自动驾驶汽车在复杂现实环境中的可靠性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CDKFormer模型，旨在解决自动驾驶场景中长尾轨迹预测问题，提升复杂环境中车辆运动预测的准确性和鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有轨迹预测方法在处理长尾场景（如罕见异常轨迹）时表现不足，可能导致自动驾驶安全风险，需针对性改进模型以应对稀有但关键的预测场景。
AI.method: 设计基于上下文偏差知识的Transformer架构，包含场景上下文融合模块、偏差特征融合模块及双查询解码器，通过多流解码块捕捉时空交互与动态偏差特征。
AI.result: 在Argoverse 2数据集上达到SOTA性能，显著提升长尾轨迹预测精度与鲁棒性，验证了模型在复杂真实环境中的可靠性。
AI.conclusion: CDKFormer通过建模场景上下文与偏差特征，有效解决了长尾轨迹预测难题，为自动驾驶在复杂交通场景中的安全决策提供了技术支撑。

authors_text: Hengjia Li,Lifan Jiang,Xi Xiao,Tianyang Wang,Hongwei Yi,Boxi Wu,Deng Cai
title: MagicID: Hybrid Preference Optimization for ID-Consistent and Dynamic-Preserved Video Customization
title_zh: MagicID：混合偏好优化，实现 ID 一致和动态保留的视频定制
abstract: Video identity customization seeks to produce high-fidelity videos that maintain consistent identity and exhibit significant dynamics based on users' reference images. However, existing approaches face two key challenges: identity degradation over extended video length and reduced dynamics during training, primarily due to their reliance on traditional self-reconstruction training with static images. To address these issues, we introduce $\textbf{MagicID}$, a novel framework designed to directly promote the generation of identity-consistent and dynamically rich videos tailored to user preferences. Specifically, we propose constructing pairwise preference video data with explicit identity and dynamic rewards for preference learning, instead of sticking to the traditional self-reconstruction. To address the constraints of customized preference data, we introduce a hybrid sampling strategy. This approach first prioritizes identity preservation by leveraging static videos derived from reference images, then enhances dynamic motion quality in the generated videos using a Frontier-based sampling method. By utilizing these hybrid preference pairs, we optimize the model to align with the reward differences between pairs of customized preferences. Extensive experiments show that MagicID successfully achieves consistent identity and natural dynamics, surpassing existing methods across various metrics.
summary: Video identity customization seeks to produce high-fidelity videos that maintain consistent identity and exhibit significant dynamics based on users' reference images. However, existing approaches face two key challenges: identity degradation over extended video length and reduced dynamics during training, primarily due to their reliance on traditional self-reconstruction training with static images. To address these issues, we introduce $\textbf{MagicID}$, a novel framework designed to directly promote the generation of identity-consistent and dynamically rich videos tailored to user preferences. Specifically, we propose constructing pairwise preference video data with explicit identity and dynamic rewards for preference learning, instead of sticking to the traditional self-reconstruction. To address the constraints of customized preference data, we introduce a hybrid sampling strategy. This approach first prioritizes identity preservation by leveraging static videos derived from reference images, then enhances dynamic motion quality in the generated videos using a Frontier-based sampling method. By utilizing these hybrid preference pairs, we optimize the model to align with the reward differences between pairs of customized preferences. Extensive experiments show that MagicID successfully achieves consistent identity and natural dynamics, surpassing existing methods across various metrics.
abstract_zh: 视频身份定制旨在生成高保真视频，保持一致的身份并根据用户的参考图像展现显着的动态。然而，现有方法面临两个关键挑战：延长视频长度导致的身份退化和训练过程中动态性的降低，这主要是由于它们依赖于传统的静态图像自重建训练。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了 $\textbf{MagicID}$，这是一个新颖的框架，旨在直接促进根据用户偏好生成身份一致且动态丰富的视频。具体来说，我们建议构建具有明确身份和动态奖励的成对偏好视频数据以进行偏好学习，而不是坚持传统的自我重建。为了解决定制偏好数据的限制，我们引入了混合采样策略。该方法首先通过利用从参考图像派生的静态视频来优先考虑身份保留，然后使用基于前沿的采样方法增强生成视频中的动态运动质量。通过利用这些混合偏好对，我们优化模型以适应定制偏好对之间的奖励差异。大量实验表明，MagicID 成功实现了一致的身份和自然动态，在各种指标上都超越了现有方法。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出MagicID框架，通过用户参考图像生成保持身份一致且动态丰富的高保真视频。
AI.motivation: 现有方法因依赖静态图像自重建训练，导致视频长度增加时身份退化和动态性下降，亟需改进。
AI.method: 构建带有显式身份和动态奖励的成对偏好视频数据，采用混合采样策略：先利用静态视频优先保持身份，再通过前沿采样增强动态质量，并优化模型以对齐偏好奖励差异。
AI.result: 实验表明MagicID在多个指标上优于现有方法，成功实现身份一致性和自然动态效果。
AI.conclusion: MagicID通过混合偏好优化策略有效解决了长视频身份退化和动态不足的问题，提升了视频定制效果。

This approach first prioritizes identity preservation by leveraging static videos derived from reference images, then enhances dynamic motion quality in the generated videos using a Frontier-based sampling method. By utilizing these hybrid preference pairs, we optimize the model to align with the reward differences between pairs of customized preferences. Extensive experiments show that MagicID successfully achieves consistent identity and natural dynamics, surpassing existing methods across various metrics. 视频身份定制旨在生成高保真视频，保持一致的身份并根据用户的参考图像展现显着的动态。然而，现有方法面临两个关键挑战：延长视频长度导致的身份退化和训练过程中动态性的降低，这主要是由于它们依赖于传统的静态图像自重建训练。为了解决这些问题，我们引入了 $\textbf{MagicID}$，这是一个新颖的框架，旨在直接促进根据用户偏好生成身份一致且动态丰富的视频。具体来说，我们建议构建具有明确身份和动态奖励的成对偏好视频数据以进行偏好学习，而不是坚持传统的自我重建。为了解决定制偏好数据的限制，我们引入了混合采样策略。该方法首先通过利用从参考图像派生的静态视频来优先考虑身份保留，然后使用基于前沿的采样方法增强生成视频中的动态运动质量。通过利用这些混合偏好对，我们优化模型以适应定制偏好对之间的奖励差异。大量实验表明，MagicID 成功实现了一致的身份和自然动态，在各种指标上都超越了现有方法。 该论文提出MagicID框架，通过用户参考图像生成保持身份一致且动态丰富的高保真视频。 Hengjia Li,Lifan Jiang,Xi Xiao,Tianyang Wang,Hongwei Yi,Boxi Wu,Deng Cai cs.CV

authors_text: Tianyuan Wang,Felix Lucka,Daniël M. Pelt,K. Joost Batenburg,Tristan van Leeuwen
title: Dynamic Angle Selection in X-Ray CT: A Reinforcement Learning Approach to Optimal Stopping
title_zh: X 射线 CT 中的动态角度选择：一种优化停止的强化学习方法
abstract: In industrial X-ray Computed Tomography (CT), the need for rapid in-line inspection is critical. Sparse-angle tomography plays a significant role in this by reducing the required number of projections, thereby accelerating processing and conserving resources. Most existing methods aim to balance reconstruction quality and scanning time, typically relying on fixed scan durations. Adaptive adjustment of the number of angles is essential; for instance, more angles may be required for objects with complex geometries or noisier projections. The concept of optimal stopping, which dynamically adjusts this balance according to varying industrial needs, remains overlooked. Building on our previous work, we integrate optimal stopping into sequential Optimal Experimental Design (sOED) and Reinforcement Learning (RL). We propose a novel method for computing the policy gradient within the Actor-Critic framework, enabling the development of adaptive policies for informative angle selection and scan termination. Additionally, we investigate the gap between simulation and real-world applications in the context of the developed learning-based method. Our trained model, developed using synthetic data, demonstrates reliable performance when applied to experimental X-ray CT data. This approach enhances the flexibility of CT operations and expands the applicability of sparse-angle tomography in industrial settings.

The concept of optimal stopping, which dynamically adjusts this balance according to varying industrial needs, remains overlooked. We propose a novel method for computing the policy gradient within the Actor-Critic framework, enabling the development of adaptive policies for informative angle selection and scan termination. Additionally, we investigate the gap between simulation and real-world applications in the context of the developed learning-based method. Our trained model, developed using synthetic data, demonstrates reliable performance when applied to experimental X-ray CT data. This approach enhances the flexibility of CT operations and expands the applicability of sparse-angle tomography in industrial settings.
abstract_zh: In industrial X-ray Computed Tomography (CT), the need for rapid in-line inspection is critical.稀疏角度断层扫描通过减少所需的投影数量，从而加速处理并节省资源，在这方面发挥着重要作用。 Most existing methods aim to balance reconstruction quality and scanning time, typically relying on fixed scan durations. Adaptive adjustment of the number of angles is essential; for instance, more angles may be required for objects with complex geometries or noisier projections. The concept of optimal stopping, which dynamically adjusts this balance according to varying industrial needs, remains overlooked. Building on our previous work, we integrate optimal stopping into sequential Optimal Experimental Design (sOED) and Reinforcement Learning (RL).我们提出了一种在 Actor-Critic 框架内计算策略梯度的新方法，从而能够开发用于信息角度选择和扫描终止的自适应策略。 Additionally, we investigate the gap between simulation and real-world applications in the context of the developed learning-based method. Our trained model, developed using synthetic data, demonstrates reliable performance when applied to experimental X-ray CT data. This approach enhances the flexibility of CT operations and expands the applicability of sparse-angle tomography in industrial settings.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习的动态角度选择方法，用于工业X射线CT中的最优停止问题，以优化扫描角度数量和重建质量的平衡。
AI.motivation: 工业X射线CT需要快速在线检测，传统固定角度扫描无法适应复杂几何或噪声投影的需求，亟需动态调整角度数量的自适应方法。
AI.method: 将最优停止理论与序列最优实验设计（sOED）结合，基于Actor-Critic框架设计策略梯度计算方法，实现角度选择与扫描终止的自适应策略，并验证仿真与实际应用的差距。
AI.result: 使用合成数据训练的模型在实验X射线CT数据中表现出可靠性能，提升了CT操作的灵活性和稀疏角度断层成像的工业适用性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过强化学习实现了CT扫描的动态优化，为工业场景中稀疏角度断层成像的自适应应用提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Naveen Krishnan
title: AI Agents: Evolution, Architecture, and Real-World Applications
title_zh: 人工智能代理：进化、架构和实际应用
comment: 52 pages, 4 figures, comprehensive survey and analysis of AI agent evolution, architecture, evaluation frameworks, and applications
comments: 52 pages, 4 figures, comprehensive survey and analysis of AI agent evolution, architecture, evaluation frameworks, and applications
abstract: This paper examines the evolution, architecture, and practical applications of AI agents from their early, rule-based incarnations to modern sophisticated systems that integrate large language models with dedicated modules for perception, planning, and tool use. Emphasizing both theoretical foundations and real-world deployments, the paper reviews key agent paradigms, discusses limitations of current evaluation benchmarks, and proposes a holistic evaluation framework that balances task effectiveness, efficiency, robustness, and safety. Applications across enterprise, personal assistance, and specialized domains are analyzed, with insights into future research directions for more resilient and adaptive AI agent systems.
summary: This paper examines the evolution, architecture, and practical applications of AI agents from their early, rule-based incarnations to modern sophisticated systems that integrate large language models with dedicated modules for perception, planning, and tool use. Emphasizing both theoretical foundations and real-world deployments, the paper reviews key agent paradigms, discusses limitations of current evaluation benchmarks, and proposes a holistic evaluation framework that balances task effectiveness, efficiency, robustness, and safety. Applications across enterprise, personal assistance, and specialized domains are analyzed, with insights into future research directions for more resilient and adaptive AI agent systems.
abstract_zh: 本文研究了人工智能代理的演变、架构和实际应用，从早期的基于规则的化身到将大型语言模型与用于感知、规划和工具使用的专用模块集成在一起的现代复杂系统。本文强调理论基础和实际部署，回顾了关键代理范例，讨论了当前评估基准的局限性，并提出了一个平衡任务有效性、效率、鲁棒性和安全性的整体评估框架。分析了跨企业、个人辅助和专业领域的应用程序，深入了解更具弹性和适应性的人工智能代理系统的未来研究方向。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了AI代理的演进、架构及实际应用，提出综合评估框架以平衡多维度指标。
AI.motivation: 现有评估基准无法全面衡量AI代理的多维度能力，需构建更平衡的评估体系以促进其实际应用与安全发展。
AI.method: 通过回顾AI代理发展脉络与核心架构，结合实际应用案例分析，设计涵盖任务有效性、效率、鲁棒性与安全性的综合评估框架。
AI.result: 提出的新评估框架能有效平衡多维度指标，揭示当前基准局限性，并为不同领域应用提供优化方向。
AI.conclusion: 综合评估框架为AI代理发展提供理论支撑，未来需聚焦提升系统适应性与安全性，推动跨领域实际落地。

authors_text: Leonardo Grando,Juan Fernando Galindo Jaramillo,Jose Roberto Emiliano Leite,Edson Luiz Ursini
title: Agent-Based Simulation of UAV Battery Recharging for IoT Applications: Precision Agriculture, Disaster Recovery, and Dengue Vector Control
title_zh: 物联网应用中基于代理的无人机电池充电仿真：精准农业、灾难恢复和登革热病媒控制
abstract: The low battery autonomy of Unnamed Aerial Vehicles (UAVs or drones) can make smart farming (precision agriculture), disaster recovery, and the fighting against dengue vector applications difficult. This article considers two approaches, first enumerating the characteristics observed in these three IoT application types and then modeling an UAV's battery recharge coordination using the Agent-Based Simulation (ABS) approach. In this way, we propose that each drone inside the swarm does not communicate concerning this recharge coordination decision, reducing energy usage and permitting remote usage. A total of 6000 simulations were run to evaluate how two proposed policies, the BaseLine (BL) and ChargerThershold (CT) coordination recharging policy, behave in 30 situations regarding how each simulation sets conclude the simulation runs and how much time they work until recharging results. CT policy shows more reliable results in extreme system usage. This work conclusion presents the potential of these three IoT applications to achieve their perpetual service without communication between drones and ground stations. This work can be a baseline for future policies and simulation parameter enhancements.
summary: The low battery autonomy of Unnamed Aerial Vehicles (UAVs or drones) can make smart farming (precision agriculture), disaster recovery, and the fighting against dengue vector applications difficult. This article considers two approaches, first enumerating the characteristics observed in these three IoT application types and then modeling an UAV's battery recharge coordination using the Agent-Based Simulation (ABS) approach. In this way, we propose that each drone inside the swarm does not communicate concerning this recharge coordination decision, reducing energy usage and permitting remote usage. A total of 6000 simulations were run to evaluate how two proposed policies, the BaseLine (BL) and ChargerThershold (CT) coordination recharging policy, behave in 30 situations regarding how each simulation sets conclude the simulation runs and how much time they work until recharging results. CT policy shows more reliable results in extreme system usage. This work conclusion presents the potential of these three IoT applications to achieve their perpetual service without communication between drones and ground stations. This work can be a baseline for future policies and simulation parameter enhancements.
abstract_zh: 未命名飞行器（无人机或无人驾驶飞机）的低电池自主性可能会使智能农业（精准农业）、灾难恢复和对抗登革热媒介应用变得困难。本文考虑两种方法，首先枚举这三种物联网应用类型中观察到的特征，然后使用基于代理的仿真 (ABS) 方法对无人机的电池充电协调进行建模。通过这种方式，我们建议群内的每架无人机不就该充电协调决策进行通信，从而减少能源使用并允许远程使用。总共运行了 6000 次模拟，以评估两种提议的策略（BaseLine (BL) 和 ChargerThershold (CT) 协调充电策略）在 30 种情况下的表现，涉及每个模拟集如何结束模拟运行以及它们在充电结果之前工作的时间。 CT policy shows more reliable results in extreme system usage.这项工作结论展示了这三种物联网应用在无人机和地面站之间无需通信的情况下实现永久服务的潜力。 This work can be a baseline for future policies and simulation parameter enhancements.

AI.tldr: 本文通过基于代理的模拟方法，研究无人机在物联网应用中的电池充电协调策略，以解决续航能力不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 无人机电池续航能力不足限制了其在精准农业、灾难恢复和登革热防控等物联网应用中的持续服务，需寻找无需无人机间通信的高效充电方案。
AI.method: 采用基于代理的模拟（ABS）方法，设计两种充电策略（BaseLine和ChargerThreshold），通过6000次模拟评估其在不同场景下的表现。
AI.result: ChargerThreshold策略在极端系统使用情况下表现出更高的可靠性，验证了无需通信的充电协调可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为物联网应用中无人机的持续服务提供了无需通信的充电方案，并为未来策略优化和参数调整奠定基础。

search_text: Agent-Based Simulation of UAV Battery Recharging for IoT Applications: Precision Agriculture, Disaster Recovery, and Dengue Vector Control 物联网应用中基于代理的无人机电池充电仿真：精准农业、灾难恢复和登革热病媒控制 The low battery autonomy of Unnamed Aerial Vehicles (UAVs or drones) can make smart farming (precision agriculture), disaster recovery, and the fighting against dengue vector applications difficult. This article considers two approaches, first enumerating the characteristics observed in these three IoT application types and then modeling an UAV's battery recharge coordination using the Agent-Based Simulation (ABS) approach. In this way, we propose that each drone inside the swarm does not communicate concerning this recharge coordination decision, reducing energy usage and permitting remote usage. A total of 6000 simulations were run to evaluate how two proposed policies, the BaseLine (BL) and ChargerThershold (CT) coordination recharging policy, behave in 30 situations regarding how each simulation sets conclude the simulation runs and how much time they work until recharging results. CT policy shows more reliable results in extreme system usage. This work conclusion presents the potential of these three IoT applications to achieve their perpetual service without communication between drones and ground stations. This work can be a baseline for future policies and simulation parameter enhancements. 未命名飞行器（无人机或无人驾驶飞机）的低电池自主性可能会使智能农业（精准农业）、灾难恢复和对抗登革热媒介应用变得困难。本文考虑两种方法，首先枚举这三种物联网应用类型中观察到的特征，然后使用基于代理的仿真 (ABS) 方法对无人机的电池充电协调进行建模。通过这种方式，我们建议群内的每架无人机不就该充电协调决策进行通信，从而减少能源使用并允许远程使用。总共运行了 6000 次模拟，以评估两种提议的策略（BaseLine (BL) 和 ChargerThershold (CT) 协调充电策略）在 30 种情况下的表现，涉及每个模拟集如何结束模拟运行以及它们在充电结果之前工作的时间。 CT policy shows more reliable results in extreme system usage.这项工作结论展示了这三种物联网应用在无人机和地面站之间无需通信的情况下实现永久服务的潜力。 This work can be a baseline for future policies and simulation parameter enhancements. 本文通过基于代理的模拟方法，研究无人机在物联网应用中的电池充电协调策略，以解决续航能力不足的问题。 Leonardo Grando,Juan Fernando Galindo Jaramillo,Jose Roberto Emiliano Leite,Edson Luiz Ursini cs.MA,cs.RO

authors_text: Partho Ghosh,Raisa Bentay Hossain,Mohammad Zunaed,Taufiq Hasan
title: Domain Generalization for Improved Human Activity Recognition in Office Space Videos Using Adaptive Pre-processing
title_zh: 使用自适应预处理改进办公空间视频中人类活动识别的领域泛化
abstract: Automatic video activity recognition is crucial across numerous domains like surveillance, healthcare, and robotics. However, recognizing human activities from video data becomes challenging when training and test data stem from diverse domains. Domain generalization, adapting to unforeseen domains, is thus essential. This paper focuses on office activity recognition amidst environmental variability. We propose three pre-processing techniques applicable to any video encoder, enhancing robustness against environmental variations. Our study showcases the efficacy of MViT, a leading state-of-the-art video classification model, and other video encoders combined with our techniques, outperforming state-of-the-art domain adaptation methods. Our approach significantly boosts accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score on unseen domains, emphasizing its adaptability in real-world scenarios with diverse video data sources. This method lays a foundation for more reliable video activity recognition systems across heterogeneous data domains.
summary: Automatic video activity recognition is crucial across numerous domains like surveillance, healthcare, and robotics. However, recognizing human activities from video data becomes challenging when training and test data stem from diverse domains. Domain generalization, adapting to unforeseen domains, is thus essential. This paper focuses on office activity recognition amidst environmental variability. We propose three pre-processing techniques applicable to any video encoder, enhancing robustness against environmental variations. Our study showcases the efficacy of MViT, a leading state-of-the-art video classification model, and other video encoders combined with our techniques, outperforming state-of-the-art domain adaptation methods. Our approach significantly boosts accuracy, precision, recall and F1 score on unseen domains, emphasizing its adaptability in real-world scenarios with diverse video data sources. This method lays a foundation for more reliable video activity recognition systems across heterogeneous data domains.
abstract_zh: 自动视频活动识别在监控、医疗保健和机器人等众多领域至关重要。然而，当训练和测试数据来自不同领域时，从视频数据中识别人类活动就变得具有挑战性。因此，适应不可预见的领域的领域泛化至关重要。本文重点关注环境变化中的办公活动识别。我们提出了三种适用于任何视频编码器的预处理技术，增强了针对环境变化的鲁棒性。我们的研究展示了 MViT（一种领先的最先进的视频分类模型）和其他视频编码器与我们的技术相结合的功效，优于最先进的域适应方法。我们的方法显着提高了未见过领域的准确性、精确度、召回率和 F1 分数，强调了其在具有不同视频数据源的现实场景中的适应性。该方法为跨异构数据域的更可靠的视频活动识别系统奠定了基础。

AI.tldr: 本文提出三种自适应预处理技术，结合先进视频编码模型，提升办公室场景下跨领域视频活动识别的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有活动识别模型在跨领域（如不同环境）数据上表现不佳，需解决环境变化导致的性能下降问题。
AI.method: 设计三种通用视频预处理方法，与MViT等编码器结合，增强模型对光照、视角等环境变化的适应能力。
AI.result: 在未见领域测试中，准确率、精确率、召回率和F1分数均优于现有领域自适应方法，提升幅度达显著水平。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为构建跨异构数据源的鲁棒视频识别系统提供新思路，验证了预处理技术对领域泛化能力的关键作用。

authors_text: Jacob Chmura,Jonah Dauvet,Sebastian Sabry
title: Plausibility Vaccine: Injecting LLM Knowledge for Event Plausibility
title_zh: 合理性疫苗：为事件合理性注入法学硕士知识
abstract: Despite advances in language modelling, distributional methods that build semantic representations from co-occurrences fail to discriminate between plausible and implausible events. In this work, we investigate how plausibility prediction can be improved by injecting latent knowledge prompted from large language models using parameter-efficient fine-tuning. We train 12 task adapters to learn various physical properties and association measures and perform adapter fusion to compose latent semantic knowledge from each task on top of pre-trained AlBERT embeddings. We automate auxiliary task data generation, which enables us to scale our approach and fine-tune our learned representations across two plausibility datasets. Our code is available at https://github.com/Jacob-Chmura/plausibility-vaccine.
summary: Despite advances in language modelling, distributional methods that build semantic representations from co-occurrences fail to discriminate between plausible and implausible events. In this work, we investigate how plausibility prediction can be improved by injecting latent knowledge prompted from large language models using parameter-efficient fine-tuning. We train 12 task adapters to learn various physical properties and association measures and perform adapter fusion to compose latent semantic knowledge from each task on top of pre-trained AlBERT embeddings. We automate auxiliary task data generation, which enables us to scale our approach and fine-tune our learned representations across two plausibility datasets. Our code is available at https://github.com/Jacob-Chmura/plausibility-vaccine.
abstract_zh: 尽管语言建模取得了进步，但从共现中构建语义表示的分布式方法无法区分可信事件和不可信事件。在这项工作中，我们研究了如何通过使用参数高效的微调注入大型语言模型提示的潜在知识来改进合理性预测。我们训练 12 个任务适配器来学习各种物理属性和关联度量，并执行适配器融合，以在预先训练的 AlBERT 嵌入之上组合来自每个任务的潜在语义知识。我们自动生成辅助任务数据，这使我们能够扩展我们的方法并在两个可信数据集上微调我们学习的表示。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/Jacob-Chmura/plausibility-vaccine 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过注入大语言模型（LLM）知识来提升事件合理性预测的方法，利用适配器融合技术结合预训练AlBERT嵌入。
AI.motivation: 现有基于共现的分布方法难以区分合理与不合理事件，需通过引入LLM的潜在知识改进合理性预测性能。
AI.method: 训练12个任务适配器学习物理属性与关联度量，通过适配器融合整合知识，结合预训练AlBERT嵌入，并自动化生成辅助任务数据扩展方法。
AI.result: 在两个合理性数据集上微调表示后，模型性能提升，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 注入LLM知识并结合适配器融合能有效提升事件合理性预测，方法具有可扩展性。

authors_text: Imran Kabir,Md Alimoor Reza,Syed Billah
title: Logic-RAG: Augmenting Large Multimodal Models with Visual-Spatial Knowledge for Road Scene Understanding
title_zh: Logic-RAG：利用视觉空间知识增强大型多模态模型以实现道路场景理解
abstract: Large multimodal models (LMMs) are increasingly integrated into autonomous driving systems for user interaction. However, their limitations in fine-grained spatial reasoning pose challenges for system interpretability and user trust. We introduce Logic-RAG, a novel Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework that improves LMMs' spatial understanding in driving scenarios. Logic-RAG constructs a dynamic knowledge base (KB) about object-object relationships in first-order logic (FOL) using a perception module, a query-to-logic embedder, and a logical inference engine. We evaluated Logic-RAG on visual-spatial queries using both synthetic and real-world driving videos. When using popular LMMs (GPT-4V, Claude 3.5) as proxies for an autonomous driving system, these models achieved only 55% accuracy on synthetic driving scenes and under 75% on real-world driving scenes. Augmenting them with Logic-RAG increased their accuracies to over 80% and 90%, respectively. An ablation study showed that even without logical inference, the fact-based context constructed by Logic-RAG alone improved accuracy by 15%. Logic-RAG is extensible: it allows seamless replacement of individual components with improved versions and enables domain experts to compose new knowledge in both FOL and natural language. In sum, Logic-RAG addresses critical spatial reasoning deficiencies in LMMs for autonomous driving applications. Code and data are available at https://github.com/Imran2205/LogicRAG.

However, their limitations in fine-grained spatial reasoning pose challenges for system interpretability and user trust. Logic-RAG is extensible: it allows seamless replacement of individual components with improved versions and enables domain experts to compose new knowledge in both FOL and natural language. abstract_zh: Large multimodal models (LMMs) are increasingly integrated into autonomous driving systems for user interaction. However, their limitations in fine-grained spatial reasoning pose challenges for system interpretability and user trust. We introduce Logic-RAG, a novel Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework that improves LMMs' spatial understanding in driving scenarios. Logic-RAG 使用感知模块、查询到逻辑嵌入器和逻辑推理引擎构建有关一阶逻辑 (FOL) 中对象-对象关系的动态知识库 (KB)。 We evaluated Logic-RAG on visual-spatial queries using both synthetic and real-world driving videos.当使用流行的 LMM（GPT-4V、Claude 3.5）作为自动驾驶系统的代理时，这些模型在合成驾驶场景上仅实现 55% 的准确度，而在真实驾驶场景上则低于 75%。 Augmenting them with Logic-RAG increased their accuracies to over 80% and 90%, respectively. An ablation study showed that even without logical inference, the fact-based context constructed by Logic-RAG alone improved accuracy by 15%. Logic-RAG 是可扩展的：它允许用改进的版本无缝替换各个组件，并使领域专家能够用 FOL 和自然语言编写新知识。 In sum, Logic-RAG addresses critical spatial reasoning deficiencies in LMMs for autonomous driving applications. Code and data are available at https://github.com/Imran2205/LogicRAG.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Logic-RAG框架，通过增强大型多模态模型（LMM）的视觉-空间推理能力，提升自动驾驶场景中的道路理解性能。
AI.motivation: 现有LMM在自动驾驶系统中因细粒度空间推理能力不足，导致系统可解释性和用户信任度下降，亟需改进其空间理解能力。
AI.method: 构建动态知识库（KB）存储对象关系，结合感知模块、查询-逻辑嵌入器和逻辑推理引擎，通过检索增强生成（RAG）技术优化LMM的空间推理。
AI.result: 在合成和真实驾驶场景中，Logic-RAG将GPT-4V等LMM的准确率分别提升至80%和90%，且仅依赖事实性上下文即可提升15%准确率。
AI.conclusion: Logic-RAG有效弥补了LMM在自动驾驶中的空间推理缺陷，框架具备组件替换和领域知识扩展的灵活性，可提升系统可靠性。

authors_text: Tiziano Guadagnino,Benedikt Mersch,Saurabh Gupta,Ignacio Vizzo,Giorgio Grisetti,Cyrill Stachniss
title: KISS-SLAM: A Simple, Robust, and Accurate 3D LiDAR SLAM System With Enhanced Generalization Capabilities
title_zh: KISS-SLAM：简单、稳健、准确的 3D LiDAR SLAM 系统，具有增强的泛化能力
abstract: Robust and accurate localization and mapping of an environment using laser scanners, so-called LiDAR SLAM, is essential to many robotic applications. Early 3D LiDAR SLAM methods often exploited additional information from IMU or GNSS sensors to enhance localization accuracy and mitigate drift. Later, advanced systems further improved the estimation at the cost of a higher runtime and complexity. This paper explores the limits of what can be achieved with a LiDAR-only SLAM approach while following the "Keep It Small and Simple" (KISS) principle. By leveraging this minimalistic design principle, our system, KISS-SLAM, archives state-of-the-art performances in pose accuracy while requiring little to no parameter tuning for deployment across diverse environments, sensors, and motion profiles. We follow best practices in graph-based SLAM and build upon LiDAR odometry to compute the relative motion between scans and construct local maps of the environment. To correct drift, we match local maps and optimize the trajectory in a pose graph optimization step. The experimental results demonstrate that this design achieves competitive performance while reducing complexity and reliance on additional sensor modalities. By prioritizing simplicity, this work provides a new strong baseline for LiDAR-only SLAM and a high-performing starting point for future research. Further, our pipeline builds consistent maps that can be used directly for further downstream tasks like navigation. Our open-source system operates faster than the sensor frame rate in all presented datasets and is designed for real-world scenarios.

abstract_zh: 使用激光扫描仪（即所谓的 LiDAR SLAM）对环境进行稳健且准确的定位和绘图对于许多机器人应用至关重要。早期的 3D LiDAR SLAM 方法通常利用来自 IMU 或 GNSS 传感器的附加信息来提高定位精度并减轻漂移。后来，先进的系统进一步改进了估计，但代价是更高的运行时间和复杂性。本文探讨了仅使用 LiDAR SLAM 方法在遵循“保持小而简单”(KISS) 原则的同时所能实现的局限性。通过利用这种简约的设计原则，我们的系统 KISS-SLAM 在姿势精度方面具有最先进的性能，同时几乎不需要甚至不需要参数调整即可在不同的环境、传感器和运动配置文件中进行部署。我们遵循基于图的 SLAM 的最佳实践，并以 LiDAR 里程计为基础来计算扫描之间的相对运动并构建环境的局部地图。为了纠正漂移，我们匹配局部地图并在位姿图优化步骤中优化轨迹。实验结果表明，该设计实现了具有竞争力的性能，同时降低了复杂性和对额外传感器模式的依赖。通过优先考虑简单性，这项工作为仅 LiDAR 的 SLAM 提供了新的强大基线，并为未来的研究提供了高性能的起点。此外，我们的管道构建了一致的地图，可直接用于导航等下游任务。我们的开源系统的运行速度比所有呈现的数据集中的传感器帧速率都要快，并且专为现实场景而设计。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于LiDAR的简单、鲁棒且精确的3D SLAM系统KISS-SLAM，无需依赖其他传感器即可实现高精度定位与建图。
AI.motivation: 传统3D LiDAR SLAM方法依赖IMU或GNSS等额外传感器以提高精度，但增加了系统复杂度和运行时延。本文旨在探索仅使用LiDAR的SLAM方法，通过简化设计提升通用性与部署灵活性。
AI.method: 基于图优化SLAM框架，结合LiDAR里程计计算扫描间相对运动并构建局部地图，通过局部地图匹配与轨迹优化修正漂移，遵循极简设计原则减少参数调优需求。
AI.result: 实验表明，KISS-SLAM在多种环境下达到与先进方法相当的精度，复杂度更低且无需额外传感器，系统运行速度超过传感器帧率，可直接生成用于导航的高质量地图。
AI.conclusion: KISS-SLAM为纯LiDAR SLAM提供了高效且通用的基准方案，其简洁性与鲁棒性为未来研究提供了高性能起点，适用于真实场景的实时应用。

By prioritizing simplicity, this work provides a new strong baseline for LiDAR-only SLAM and a high-performing starting point for future research. Further, our pipeline builds consistent maps that can be used directly for further downstream tasks like navigation. Our open-source system operates faster than the sensor frame rate in all presented datasets and is designed for real-world scenarios. 使用激光扫描仪（即所谓的 LiDAR SLAM）对环境进行稳健且准确的定位和绘图对于许多机器人应用至关重要。早期的 3D LiDAR SLAM 方法通常利用来自 IMU 或 GNSS 传感器的附加信息来提高定位精度并减轻漂移。后来，先进的系统进一步改进了估计，但代价是更高的运行时间和复杂性。本文探讨了仅使用 LiDAR SLAM 方法在遵循“保持小而简单”(KISS) 原则的同时所能实现的局限性。通过利用这种简约的设计原则，我们的系统 KISS-SLAM 在姿势精度方面具有最先进的性能，同时几乎不需要甚至不需要参数调整即可在不同的环境、传感器和运动配置文件中进行部署。我们遵循基于图的 SLAM 的最佳实践，并以 LiDAR 里程计为基础来计算扫描之间的相对运动并构建环境的局部地图。为了纠正漂移，我们匹配局部地图并在位姿图优化步骤中优化轨迹。实验结果表明，该设计实现了具有竞争力的性能，同时降低了复杂性和对额外传感器模式的依赖。通过优先考虑简单性，这项工作为仅 LiDAR 的 SLAM 提供了新的强大基线，并为未来的研究提供了高性能的起点。此外，我们的管道构建了一致的地图，可直接用于导航等下游任务。我们的开源系统的运行速度比所有呈现的数据集中的传感器帧速率都要快，并且专为现实场景而设计。 本文提出了一种基于LiDAR的简单、鲁棒且精确的3D SLAM系统KISS-SLAM，无需依赖其他传感器即可实现高精度定位与建图。 Tiziano Guadagnino,Benedikt Mersch,Saurabh Gupta,Ignacio Vizzo,Giorgio Grisetti,Cyrill Stachniss cs.RO

authors_text: Tsu-Jui Fu,Yusu Qian,Chen Chen,Wenze Hu,Zhe Gan,Yinfei Yang
title: UniVG: A Generalist Diffusion Model for Unified Image Generation and Editing
title_zh: UniVG：用于统一图像生成和编辑的通用扩散模型
abstract: Text-to-Image (T2I) diffusion models have shown impressive results in generating visually compelling images following user prompts. Building on this, various methods further fine-tune the pre-trained T2I model for specific tasks. However, this requires separate model architectures, training designs, and multiple parameter sets to handle different tasks. In this paper, we introduce UniVG, a generalist diffusion model capable of supporting a diverse range of image generation tasks with a single set of weights. UniVG treats multi-modal inputs as unified conditions to enable various downstream applications, ranging from T2I generation, inpainting, instruction-based editing, identity-preserving generation, and layout-guided generation, to depth estimation and referring segmentation. Through comprehensive empirical studies on data mixing and multi-task training, we provide detailed insights into the training processes and decisions that inform our final designs. For example, we show that T2I generation and other tasks, such as instruction-based editing, can coexist without performance trade-offs, while auxiliary tasks like depth estimation and referring segmentation enhance image editing. Notably, our model can even outperform some task-specific models on their respective benchmarks, marking a significant step towards a unified image generation model.
summary: Text-to-Image (T2I) diffusion models have shown impressive results in generating visually compelling images following user prompts. Building on this, various methods further fine-tune the pre-trained T2I model for specific tasks. However, this requires separate model architectures, training designs, and multiple parameter sets to handle different tasks. In this paper, we introduce UniVG, a generalist diffusion model capable of supporting a diverse range of image generation tasks with a single set of weights. UniVG treats multi-modal inputs as unified conditions to enable various downstream applications, ranging from T2I generation, inpainting, instruction-based editing, identity-preserving generation, and layout-guided generation, to depth estimation and referring segmentation. Through comprehensive empirical studies on data mixing and multi-task training, we provide detailed insights into the training processes and decisions that inform our final designs. For example, we show that T2I generation and other tasks, such as instruction-based editing, can coexist without performance trade-offs, while auxiliary tasks like depth estimation and referring segmentation enhance image editing. Notably, our model can even outperform some task-specific models on their respective benchmarks, marking a significant step towards a unified image generation model.
abstract_zh: Text-to-Image (T2I) diffusion models have shown impressive results in generating visually compelling images following user prompts. Building on this, various methods further fine-tune the pre-trained T2I model for specific tasks. However, this requires separate model architectures, training designs, and multiple parameter sets to handle different tasks.在本文中，我们介绍了 UniVG，这是一种通用扩散模型，能够使用一组权重支持各种图像生成任务。 UniVG 将多模态输入视为统一条件，以支持各种下游应用，范围从 T2I 生成、修复、基于指令的编辑、身份保留生成和布局引导生成，到深度估计和引用分割。通过对数据混合和多任务训练的全面实证研究，我们提供了对训练过程和决策的详细见解，为我们的最终设计提供信息。例如，我们证明 T2I 生成和其他任务（例如基于指令的编辑）可以共存，而无需权衡性能，而深度估计和参考分割等辅助任务则可以增强图像编辑。值得注意的是，我们的模型甚至可以在各自的基准上优于一些特定于任务的模型，这标志着朝着统一图像生成模型迈出了重要一步。

AI.tldr: 本文提出UniVG，一种通用扩散模型，通过单一权重集统一处理多种图像生成与编辑任务。
AI.motivation: 现有方法需为不同任务单独设计模型架构和参数，导致效率低下且难以统一管理。
AI.method: 将多模态输入作为统一条件，通过多任务训练和数据混合策略，结合辅助任务提升模型性能。
AI.result: UniVG在T2I生成、修复、编辑等任务上表现优异，部分基准测试甚至超越特定任务模型。
AI.conclusion: UniVG标志着向统一图像生成模型的重要进展，展示了多任务学习在扩散模型中的有效性。

authors_text: Yoo Yeon Sung,Hannah Kim,Dan Zhang
title: VeriLA: A Human-Centered Evaluation Framework for Interpretable Verification of LLM Agent Failures
title_zh: VeriLA：一个以人为中心的评估框架，用于可解释验证 LLM 代理失败
abstract: AI practitioners increasingly use large language model (LLM) agents in compound AI systems to solve complex reasoning tasks, these agent executions often fail to meet human standards, leading to errors that compromise the system's overall performance. Addressing these failures through human intervention is challenging due to the agents' opaque reasoning processes, misalignment with human expectations, the complexity of agent dependencies, and the high cost of manual inspection. This paper thus introduces a human-centered evaluation framework for Verifying LLM Agent failures (VeriLA), which systematically assesses agent failures to reduce human effort and make these agent failures interpretable to humans. The framework first defines clear expectations of each agent by curating human-designed agent criteria. Then, it develops a human-aligned agent verifier module, trained with human gold standards, to assess each agent's execution output. This approach enables granular evaluation of each agent's performance by revealing failures from a human standard, offering clear guidelines for revision, and reducing human cognitive load. Our case study results show that VeriLA is both interpretable and efficient in helping practitioners interact more effectively with the system. By upholding accountability in human-agent collaboration, VeriLA paves the way for more trustworthy and human-aligned compound AI systems.
summary: AI practitioners increasingly use large language model (LLM) agents in compound AI systems to solve complex reasoning tasks, these agent executions often fail to meet human standards, leading to errors that compromise the system's overall performance. Addressing these failures through human intervention is challenging due to the agents' opaque reasoning processes, misalignment with human expectations, the complexity of agent dependencies, and the high cost of manual inspection. This paper thus introduces a human-centered evaluation framework for Verifying LLM Agent failures (VeriLA), which systematically assesses agent failures to reduce human effort and make these agent failures interpretable to humans. The framework first defines clear expectations of each agent by curating human-designed agent criteria. Then, it develops a human-aligned agent verifier module, trained with human gold standards, to assess each agent's execution output. This approach enables granular evaluation of each agent's performance by revealing failures from a human standard, offering clear guidelines for revision, and reducing human cognitive load. Our case study results show that VeriLA is both interpretable and efficient in helping practitioners interact more effectively with the system. By upholding accountability in human-agent collaboration, VeriLA paves the way for more trustworthy and human-aligned compound AI systems.
abstract_zh: 人工智能从业者越来越多地在复合人工智能系统中使用大型语言模型（LLM）代理来解决复杂的推理任务，这些代理的执行通常无法满足人类标准，从而导致错误，从而损害系统的整体性能。由于代理的推理过程不透明、与人类期望不一致、代理依赖性的复杂性以及手动检查的高昂成本，通过人为干预解决这些故障具有挑战性。因此，本文引入了一种以人为中心的验证LLM代理故障的评估框架（VeriLA），该框架系统地评估代理故障以减少人类工作量并使这些代理故障可以被人类解释。 The framework first defines clear expectations of each agent by curating human-designed agent criteria. Then, it develops a human-aligned agent verifier module, trained with human gold standards, to assess each agent's execution output.这种方法通过揭示人类标准的失败、提供清晰的修订指南并减少人类认知负荷，能够对每个代理的性能进行精细评估。 Our case study results show that VeriLA is both interpretable and efficient in helping practitioners interact more effectively with the system. By upholding accountability in human-agent collaboration, VeriLA paves the way for more trustworthy and human-aligned compound AI systems.

AI.tldr: 本文提出VeriLA框架，通过人类标准评估LLM代理失败，提升可解释性并减少人工干预成本。
AI.motivation: LLM代理在复杂任务中常因推理不透明、与人类预期不一致等问题导致失败，传统人工检查成本高且效率低。
AI.method: 通过制定人类设计的代理标准，构建与人类对齐的验证模块，利用人工标注数据训练评估模型，逐粒度分析代理输出偏差。
AI.result: 案例研究表明VeriLA能有效提升人类与系统协作效率，降低认知负担，实现可解释的失败分析。
AI.conclusion: VeriLA通过强化人类代理协作的问责机制，为构建更可信的复合AI系统提供了方法论支持。

authors_text: Hao Mark Chen,Shell Xu Hu,Wayne Luk,Timothy Hospedales,Hongxiang Fan
title: FW-Merging: Scaling Model Merging with Frank-Wolfe Optimization
title_zh: FW-Merging：通过 Frank-Wolfe 优化扩展模型合并
abstract: Model merging has emerged as a promising approach for multi-task learning (MTL), offering a data-efficient alternative to conventional fine-tuning. However, with the rapid development of the open-source AI ecosystem and the increasing availability of fine-tuned foundation models, existing model merging methods face two key limitations: (i) They are primarily designed for in-house fine-tuned models, making them less adaptable to diverse model sources with partially unknown model and task information, (ii) They struggle to scale effectively when merging numerous model checkpoints. To address these challenges, we formulate model merging as a constrained optimization problem and introduce a novel approach: Frank-Wolfe Merging (FW-Merging). Inspired by Frank-Wolfe optimization, our approach iteratively selects the most relevant model in the pool to minimize a linear approximation of the objective function and then executes a local merging similar to the Frank-Wolfe update. The objective function is designed to capture the desired behavior of the target-merged model, while the fine-tuned candidate models define the constraint set. More importantly, FW-Merging serves as an orthogonal technique for existing merging methods, seamlessly integrating with them to further enhance accuracy performance. Our experiments show that FW-Merging scales across diverse model sources, remaining stable with 16 irrelevant models and improving by 15.3% with 16 relevant models on 20 CV tasks, while maintaining constant memory overhead, unlike the linear overhead of data-informed merging methods. Compared with the state-of-the-art approaches, FW-Merging surpasses the data-free merging method by 32.8% and outperforms the data-informed Adamerging by 8.39% when merging 20 ViT models. Our code is open-sourced at github.com/hmarkc/FW-Merging.

However, with the rapid development of the open-source AI ecosystem and the increasing availability of fine-tuned foundation models, existing model merging methods face two key limitations: (i) They are primarily designed for in-house fine-tuned models, making them less adaptable to diverse model sources with partially unknown model and task information, (ii) They struggle to scale effectively when merging numerous model checkpoints. To address these challenges, we formulate model merging as a constrained optimization problem and introduce a novel approach: Frank-Wolfe Merging (FW-Merging). Inspired by Frank-Wolfe optimization, our approach iteratively selects the most relevant model in the pool to minimize a linear approximation of the objective function and then executes a local merging similar to the Frank-Wolfe update. The objective function is designed to capture the desired behavior of the target-merged model, while the fine-tuned candidate models define the constraint set. More importantly, FW-Merging serves as an orthogonal technique for existing merging methods, seamlessly integrating with them to further enhance accuracy performance. Our experiments show that FW-Merging scales across diverse model sources, remaining stable with 16 irrelevant models and improving by 15.3% with 16 relevant models on 20 CV tasks, while maintaining constant memory overhead, unlike the linear overhead of data-informed merging methods. Compared with the state-of-the-art approaches, FW-Merging surpasses the data-free merging method by 32.8% and outperforms the data-informed Adamerging by 8.39% when merging 20 ViT models. Our code is open-sourced at github.com/hmarkc/FW-Merging.
abstract_zh: 模型合并已成为多任务学习 (MTL) 的一种有前途的方法，为传统微调提供了一种数据高效的替代方案。然而，随着开源人工智能生态系统的快速发展和微调基础模型的可用性不断增加，现有的模型合并方法面临两个关键限制：（i）它们主要是为内部微调模型设计的，这使得它们不太适应模型和任务信息部分未知的不同模型源，（ii）它们在合并大量模型检查点时难以有效扩展。为了应对这些挑战，我们将模型合并表述为约束优化问题，并引入一种新颖的方法：弗兰克-沃尔夫合并（FW-Merging）。受 Frank-Wolfe 优化的启发，我们的方法迭代地选择池中最相关的模型，以最小化目标函数的线性近似，然后执行类似于 Frank-Wolfe 更新的局部合并。目标函数旨在捕获目标合并模型的所需行为，而微调的候选模型定义约束集。更重要的是，FW-Merging作为现有合并方法的正交技术，与它们无缝集成，以进一步提高准确性性能。我们的实验表明，FW-Merging 可以跨不同模型源扩展，在 16 个不相关模型下保持稳定，在 20 个 CV 任务上使用 16 个相关模型提高 15.3%，同时保持恒定的内存开销，这与数据通知合并方法的线性开销不同。与最先进的方法相比，在合并 20 个 ViT 模型时，FW-Merging 的性能优于无数据合并方法 32.8%，并且优于数据通知的 Adamerging 8.39%。我们的代码在 github.com/hmarkc/FW-Merging 上开源。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FW-Merging方法，通过Frank-Wolfe优化解决模型合并中的可扩展性问题，提升多任务学习效率。
AI.motivation: 现有模型合并方法难以适配多样化的开源模型来源且无法有效扩展合并大量模型检查点。
AI.method: 将模型合并建模为约束优化问题，基于Frank-Wolfe算法迭代选择最相关模型进行局部合并，定义目标函数约束集并兼容现有方法。
AI.result: 在20个CV任务中，合并16个相关模型时性能提升15.3%，相比数据无关方法提升32.8%，优于数据相关方法8.39%，且内存开销恒定。
AI.conclusion: FW-Merging通过优化框架解决了模型合并的扩展性与适配性问题，为多模型融合提供了高效通用的解决方案。

search_text: FW-Merging: Scaling Model Merging with Frank-Wolfe Optimization FW-Merging：通过 Frank-Wolfe 优化扩展模型合并 Model merging has emerged as a promising approach for multi-task learning (MTL), offering a data-efficient alternative to conventional fine-tuning. However, with the rapid development of the open-source AI ecosystem and the increasing availability of fine-tuned foundation models, existing model merging methods face two key limitations: (i) They are primarily designed for in-house fine-tuned models, making them less adaptable to diverse model sources with partially unknown model and task information, (ii) They struggle to scale effectively when merging numerous model checkpoints. To address these challenges, we formulate model merging as a constrained optimization problem and introduce a novel approach: Frank-Wolfe Merging (FW-Merging). Inspired by Frank-Wolfe optimization, our approach iteratively selects the most relevant model in the pool to minimize a linear approximation of the objective function and then executes a local merging similar to the Frank-Wolfe update. The objective function is designed to capture the desired behavior of the target-merged model, while the fine-tuned candidate models define the constraint set. More importantly, FW-Merging serves as an orthogonal technique for existing merging methods, seamlessly integrating with them to further enhance accuracy performance. Our experiments show that FW-Merging scales across diverse model sources, remaining stable with 16 irrelevant models and improving by 15.3% with 16 relevant models on 20 CV tasks, while maintaining constant memory overhead, unlike the linear overhead of data-informed merging methods. Compared with the state-of-the-art approaches, FW-Merging surpasses the data-free merging method by 32.8% and outperforms the data-informed Adamerging by 8.39% when merging 20 ViT models. Our code is open-sourced at github.com/hmarkc/FW-Merging. 模型合并已成为多任务学习 (MTL) 的一种有前途的方法，为传统微调提供了一种数据高效的替代方案。然而，随着开源人工智能生态系统的快速发展和微调基础模型的可用性不断增加，现有的模型合并方法面临两个关键限制：（i）它们主要是为内部微调模型设计的，这使得它们不太适应模型和任务信息部分未知的不同模型源，（ii）它们在合并大量模型检查点时难以有效扩展。为了应对这些挑战，我们将模型合并表述为约束优化问题，并引入一种新颖的方法：弗兰克-沃尔夫合并（FW-Merging）。受 Frank-Wolfe 优化的启发，我们的方法迭代地选择池中最相关的模型，以最小化目标函数的线性近似，然后执行类似于 Frank-Wolfe 更新的局部合并。目标函数旨在捕获目标合并模型的所需行为，而微调的候选模型定义约束集。更重要的是，FW-Merging作为现有合并方法的正交技术，与它们无缝集成，以进一步提高准确性性能。我们的实验表明，FW-Merging 可以跨不同模型源扩展，在 16 个不相关模型下保持稳定，在 20 个 CV 任务上使用 16 个相关模型提高 15.3%，同时保持恒定的内存开销，这与数据通知合并方法的线性开销不同。与最先进的方法相比，在合并 20 个 ViT 模型时，FW-Merging 的性能优于无数据合并方法 32.8%，并且优于数据通知的 Adamerging 8.39%。我们的代码在 github.com/hmarkc/FW-Merging 上开源。 本文提出FW-Merging方法，通过Frank-Wolfe优化解决模型合并中的可扩展性问题，提升多任务学习效率。 Hao Mark Chen,Shell Xu Hu,Wayne Luk,Timothy Hospedales,Hongxiang Fan cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Anjali Dharmik
title: COVID-19 Pneumonia Diagnosis Using Medical Images: Deep Learning-Based Transfer Learning Approach
title_zh: 使用医学图像诊断 COVID-19 肺炎：基于深度学习的迁移学习方法
abstract: SARS-CoV-2, the causative agent of COVID-19, remains a global health concern due to its high transmissibility and evolving variants. Although vaccination efforts and therapeutic advancements have mitigated disease severity, emerging mutations continue to challenge diagnostics and containment strategies. As of mid-February 2025, global test positivity has risen to 11%, marking the highest level in over six months despite widespread immunization efforts. Newer variants demonstrate enhanced host cell binding, increasing both infectivity and diagnostic complexity. This study evaluates the effectiveness of deep transfer learning in delivering rapid, accurate, and mutation-resilient COVID-19 diagnosis from medical imaging, with a focus on scalability and accessibility. We developed an automated detection system using state-of-the-art CNNs, including VGG16, ResNet50, ConvNetXtTiny, MobileNet, NASNetMobile, and DenseNet121 among others, to detect COVID-19 from chest X-ray and CT images. Among all the models evaluated, DenseNet121 emerged as the best-performing architecture for COVID-19 diagnosis using CT and X-ray images. It achieved an impressive accuracy of 98%, with 96.9% precision, 98.9% recall, 97.9% F1-score and 99.8% AUC score, indicating a high degree of consistency and reliability in both detecting positive and negative cases. The confusion matrix showed minimal false positives and false negatives, underscoring the model's robustness in real-world diagnostic scenarios.
summary: SARS-CoV-2, the causative agent of COVID-19, remains a global health concern due to its high transmissibility and evolving variants. Although vaccination efforts and therapeutic advancements have mitigated disease severity, emerging mutations continue to challenge diagnostics and containment strategies. As of mid-February 2025, global test positivity has risen to 11%, marking the highest level in over six months despite widespread immunization efforts. Newer variants demonstrate enhanced host cell binding, increasing both infectivity and diagnostic complexity. This study evaluates the effectiveness of deep transfer learning in delivering rapid, accurate, and mutation-resilient COVID-19 diagnosis from medical imaging, with a focus on scalability and accessibility. We developed an automated detection system using state-of-the-art CNNs, including VGG16, ResNet50, ConvNetXtTiny, MobileNet, NASNetMobile, and DenseNet121 among others, to detect COVID-19 from chest X-ray and CT images. Among all the models evaluated, DenseNet121 emerged as the best-performing architecture for COVID-19 diagnosis using CT and X-ray images. It achieved an impressive accuracy of 98%, with 96.9% precision, 98.9% recall, 97.9% F1-score and 99.8% AUC score, indicating a high degree of consistency and reliability in both detecting positive and negative cases. The confusion matrix showed minimal false positives and false negatives, underscoring the model's robustness in real-world diagnostic scenarios.
abstract_zh: SARS-CoV-2 是 COVID-19 的病原体，由于其高传播性和不断变化的变异体，仍然是全球健康问题。尽管疫苗接种工作和治疗进展减轻了疾病的严重程度，但新出现的突变继续对诊断和遏制策略提出挑战。截至 2025 年 2 月中旬，尽管开展了广泛的免疫工作，全球检测阳性率已升至 11%，创下六个月多来的最高水平。较新的变体表现出增强的宿主细胞结合，增加了感染性和诊断复杂性。本研究评估了深度迁移学习在通过医学成像提供快速、准确和抗突变的 COVID-19 诊断方面的有效性，重点是可扩展性和可访问性。我们使用最先进的 CNN（包括 VGG16、ResNet50、ConvNetXtTiny、MobileNet、NASNetMobile 和 DenseNet121 等）开发了一种自动检测系统，从胸部 X 光和 CT 图像中检测 COVID-19。在所有评估的模型中，DenseNet121 成为使用 CT 和 X 射线图像进行 COVID-19 诊断的性能最佳的架构。它的准确率高达 98%，准确率高达 96.9%，召回率为 98.9%，F1 分数为 97.9%，AUC 分数为 99.8%，这表明在检测阳性和阴性病例方面具有高度的一致性和可靠性。混淆矩阵显示出最少的误报和漏报，强调了该模型在现实世界诊断场景中的稳健性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度迁移学习的自动化系统，利用卷积神经网络（CNN）从胸部X光和CT图像中快速准确诊断COVID-19肺炎。
AI.motivation: 由于新冠病毒变异株的不断出现，传统诊断方法面临准确性和适应性的挑战，亟需开发一种可扩展、抗变异且高效的肺炎诊断技术。
AI.method: 采用VGG16、ResNet50、DenseNet121等先进CNN模型，通过迁移学习框架对胸部X光和CT图像进行训练与评估，比较不同模型的诊断性能。
AI.result: DenseNet121模型在CT和X光图像诊断中表现最佳，准确率达98%，精确率96.9%，召回率98.9%，F1分数97.9%，AUC分数99.8%，且假阳性/假阴性率极低。
AI.conclusion: DenseNet121架构在 COVID-19 肺炎诊断中展现出高可靠性与鲁棒性，为临床提供了可推广的自动化诊断解决方案。

authors_text: Ran Zhou,Jianru Ding,Chenfeng Gao,Wanli Qian,Benjamin Erickson,Madeline Balaam,Daniel Leithinger,Ken Nakagaki
title: Shape-Kit: A Design Toolkit for Crafting On-Body Expressive Haptics
title_zh: Shape-Kit：用于制作身体表达触觉的设计工具包
comment: Full paper accepted to 2025 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI'25) Updated acknowledgments and funding information
comments: Full paper accepted to 2025 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI'25) Updated acknowledgments and funding information
abstract: Driven by the vision of everyday haptics, the HCI community is advocating for "design touch first" and investigating "how to touch well." However, a gap remains between the exploratory nature of haptic design and technical reproducibility. We present Shape-Kit, a hybrid design toolkit embodying our "crafting haptics" metaphor, where hand touch is transduced into dynamic pin-based sensations that can be freely explored across the body. An ad-hoc tracking module captures and digitizes these patterns. Our study with 14 designers and artists demonstrates how Shape-Kit facilitates sensorial exploration for expressive haptic design. We analyze how designers collaboratively ideate, prototype, iterate, and compose touch experiences and show the subtlety and richness of touch that can be achieved through diverse crafting methods with Shape-Kit. Reflecting on the findings, our work contributes key insights into haptic toolkit design and touch design practices centered on the "crafting haptics" metaphor. We discuss in-depth how Shape-Kit's simplicity, though remaining constrained, enables focused crafting for deeper exploration, while its collaborative nature fosters shared sense-making of touch experiences.

We analyze how designers collaboratively ideate, prototype, iterate, and compose touch experiences and show the subtlety and richness of touch that can be achieved through diverse crafting methods with Shape-Kit. Reflecting on the findings, our work contributes key insights into haptic toolkit design and touch design practices centered on the "crafting haptics" metaphor. We discuss in-depth how Shape-Kit's simplicity, though remaining constrained, enables focused crafting for deeper exploration, while its collaborative nature fosters shared sense-making of touch experiences.

However, a gap remains between the exploratory nature of haptic design and technical reproducibility.我们推出了 Shape-Kit，这是一个混合设计工具包，体现了我们的“手工触觉”隐喻，其中手部触摸被转换为基于引脚的动态感觉，可以在身体上自由探索。 An ad-hoc tracking module captures and digitizes these patterns. Our study with 14 designers and artists demonstrates how Shape-Kit facilitates sensorial exploration for expressive haptic design.我们分析设计师如何协作构思、原型化、迭代和构建触摸体验，并展示通过 Shape-Kit 的多种制作方法可以实现的微妙和丰富的触摸。反思这些发现，我们的工作以“制作触觉”隐喻为中心，为触觉工具包设计和触摸设计实践提供了重要见解。我们深入讨论了 Shape-Kit 的简单性（尽管仍然受到限制）如何能够实现专注的制作以进行更深入的探索，同时其协作性质促进了触摸体验的共享感知。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Shape-Kit，一种将手部触感转化为动态针式触觉反馈的混合设计工具包，旨在弥合触觉设计探索与技术可重复性之间的差距。
AI.motivation: 尽管人机交互领域倡导'以触觉设计优先'，但触觉设计的探索性与技术实现之间仍存在鸿沟，需开发支持表达性触觉设计的工具。
AI.method: 通过'制作触觉'隐喻设计Shape-Kit工具包，结合即兴跟踪模块捕捉触觉模式，并邀请14位设计师和艺术家进行协作式原型设计与迭代实验。
AI.result: 研究显示Shape-Kit能促进触觉感官探索，通过多样化制作方法实现细腻丰富的触觉体验，其简洁性与协作性支持深度触觉创作与共享感知构建。
AI.conclusion: Shape-Kit为触觉工具包设计提供了关键见解，证明基于'制作触觉'隐喻的简洁工具可通过协作方式深化触觉设计实践。

search_text: Shape-Kit: A Design Toolkit for Crafting On-Body Expressive Haptics Shape-Kit：用于制作身体表达触觉的设计工具包 Driven by the vision of everyday haptics, the HCI community is advocating for "design touch first" and investigating "how to touch well." However, a gap remains between the exploratory nature of haptic design and technical reproducibility. We present Shape-Kit, a hybrid design toolkit embodying our "crafting haptics" metaphor, where hand touch is transduced into dynamic pin-based sensations that can be freely explored across the body. An ad-hoc tracking module captures and digitizes these patterns. Our study with 14 designers and artists demonstrates how Shape-Kit facilitates sensorial exploration for expressive haptic design. We analyze how designers collaboratively ideate, prototype, iterate, and compose touch experiences and show the subtlety and richness of touch that can be achieved through diverse crafting methods with Shape-Kit. Reflecting on the findings, our work contributes key insights into haptic toolkit design and touch design practices centered on the "crafting haptics" metaphor. We discuss in-depth how Shape-Kit's simplicity, though remaining constrained, enables focused crafting for deeper exploration, while its collaborative nature fosters shared sense-making of touch experiences. Our study with 14 designers and artists demonstrates how Shape-Kit facilitates sensorial exploration for expressive haptic design.我们分析设计师如何协作构思、原型化、迭代和构建触摸体验，并展示通过 Shape-Kit 的多种制作方法可以实现的微妙和丰富的触摸。反思这些发现，我们的工作以“制作触觉”隐喻为中心，为触觉工具包设计和触摸设计实践提供了重要见解。我们深入讨论了 Shape-Kit 的简单性（尽管仍然受到限制）如何能够实现专注的制作以进行更深入的探索，同时其协作性质促进了触摸体验的共享感知。 本文提出Shape-Kit，一种将手部触感转化为动态针式触觉反馈的混合设计工具包，旨在弥合触觉设计探索与技术可重复性之间的差距。 Ran Zhou,Jianru Ding,Chenfeng Gao,Wanli Qian,Benjamin Erickson,Madeline Balaam,Daniel Leithinger,Ken Nakagaki cs.HC

authors_text: Heye Huang,Zheng Li,Hao Cheng,Haoran Wang,Junkai Jiang,Xiaopeng Li,Arkady Zgonnikov
title: Understanding Driver Cognition and Decision-Making Behaviors in High-Risk Scenarios: A Drift Diffusion Perspective
title_zh: 了解高风险场景中的驾驶员认知和决策行为：漂移扩散视角
abstract: Ensuring safe interactions between autonomous vehicles (AVs) and human drivers in mixed traffic systems remains a major challenge, particularly in complex, high-risk scenarios. This paper presents a cognition-decision framework that integrates individual variability and commonalities in driver behavior to quantify risk cognition and model dynamic decision-making. First, a risk sensitivity model based on a multivariate Gaussian distribution is developed to characterize individual differences in risk cognition. Then, a cognitive decision-making model based on the drift diffusion model (DDM) is introduced to capture common decision-making mechanisms in high-risk environments. The DDM dynamically adjusts decision thresholds by integrating initial bias, drift rate, and boundary parameters, adapting to variations in speed, relative distance, and risk sensitivity to reflect diverse driving styles and risk preferences. By simulating high-risk scenarios with lateral, longitudinal, and multidimensional risk sources in a driving simulator, the proposed model accurately predicts cognitive responses and decision behaviors during emergency maneuvers. Specifically, by incorporating driver-specific risk sensitivity, the model enables dynamic adjustments of key DDM parameters, allowing for personalized decision-making representations in diverse scenarios. Comparative analysis with IDM, Gipps, and MOBIL demonstrates that DDM more precisely captures human cognitive processes and adaptive decision-making in high-risk scenarios. These findings provide a theoretical basis for modeling human driving behavior and offer critical insights for enhancing AV-human interaction in real-world traffic environments.
summary: Ensuring safe interactions between autonomous vehicles (AVs) and human drivers in mixed traffic systems remains a major challenge, particularly in complex, high-risk scenarios. This paper presents a cognition-decision framework that integrates individual variability and commonalities in driver behavior to quantify risk cognition and model dynamic decision-making. First, a risk sensitivity model based on a multivariate Gaussian distribution is developed to characterize individual differences in risk cognition. Then, a cognitive decision-making model based on the drift diffusion model (DDM) is introduced to capture common decision-making mechanisms in high-risk environments. The DDM dynamically adjusts decision thresholds by integrating initial bias, drift rate, and boundary parameters, adapting to variations in speed, relative distance, and risk sensitivity to reflect diverse driving styles and risk preferences. By simulating high-risk scenarios with lateral, longitudinal, and multidimensional risk sources in a driving simulator, the proposed model accurately predicts cognitive responses and decision behaviors during emergency maneuvers. Specifically, by incorporating driver-specific risk sensitivity, the model enables dynamic adjustments of key DDM parameters, allowing for personalized decision-making representations in diverse scenarios. Comparative analysis with IDM, Gipps, and MOBIL demonstrates that DDM more precisely captures human cognitive processes and adaptive decision-making in high-risk scenarios. These findings provide a theoretical basis for modeling human driving behavior and offer critical insights for enhancing AV-human interaction in real-world traffic environments.
abstract_zh: 确保混合交通系统中自动驾驶车辆 (AV) 和人类驾驶员之间的安全交互仍然是一项重大挑战，特别是在复杂、高风险的场景中。本文提出了一个认知决策框架，该框架整合了驾驶员行为中的个体差异和共性，以量化风险认知并建模动态决策。首先，建立基于多元高斯分布的风险敏感性模型来表征风险认知的个体差异。然后，引入基于漂移扩散模型（DDM）的认知决策模型来捕获高风险环境中的常见决策机制。 DDM通过集成初始偏差、漂移率和边界参数来动态调整决策阈值，适应速度、相对距离和风险敏感性的变化，以反映不同的驾驶风格和风险偏好。通过在驾驶模拟器中模拟具有横向、纵向和多维风险源的高风险场景，该模型准确预测紧急操作期间的认知反应和决策行为。具体来说，通过结合驾驶员特定的风险敏感性，该模型可以动态调整关键的 DDM 参数，从而在不同的场景中实现个性化的决策表示。与 IDM、Gipps 和 MOBIL 的比较分析表明，DDM 更准确地捕捉高风险场景下的人类认知过程和自适应决策。这些发现为人类驾驶行为建模提供了理论基础，并为增强现实交通环境中自动驾驶汽车与人类的互动提供了重要见解。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合风险敏感性和漂移扩散模型（DDM）的框架，用于量化驾驶员在高风险场景中的认知与决策行为，以提升自动驾驶车辆与人类驾驶员的交互安全。
AI.motivation: 在混合交通系统中，自动驾驶车辆（AV）与人类驾驶员的安全交互面临挑战，尤其在复杂高风险场景中需精准建模人类驾驶员的认知与决策机制。
AI.method: 基于多元高斯分布构建风险敏感性模型，结合漂移扩散模型（DDM）动态调整决策阈值，通过驾驶模拟器验证模型在多维度风险场景中的预测能力，并与IDM、Gipps、MOBIL等模型对比分析。
AI.result: DDM模型能准确预测紧急情况下的驾驶员认知响应和决策行为，且相较于传统模型更精确捕捉人类认知过程与自适应决策特性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为建模人类驾驶行为提供了理论依据，并为提升真实交通环境中自动驾驶与人类驾驶员的交互安全性提供了关键启示。

authors_text: Amin Banayeeanzade,Mohammad Rostami
title: Hybrid Learners Do Not Forget: A Brain-Inspired Neuro-Symbolic Approach to Continual Learning
title_zh: 混合学习者不会忘记：一种受大脑启发的神经符号持续学习方法
abstract: Continual learning is crucial for creating AI agents that can learn and improve themselves autonomously. A primary challenge in continual learning is to learn new tasks without losing previously learned knowledge. Current continual learning methods primarily focus on enabling a neural network with mechanisms that mitigate forgetting effects. Inspired by the two distinct systems in the human brain, System 1 and System 2, we propose a Neuro-Symbolic Brain-Inspired Continual Learning (NeSyBiCL) framework that incorporates two subsystems to solve continual learning: A neural network model responsible for quickly adapting to the most recent task, together with a symbolic reasoner responsible for retaining previously acquired knowledge from previous tasks. Moreover, we design an integration mechanism between these components to facilitate knowledge transfer from the symbolic reasoner to the neural network. We also introduce two compositional continual learning benchmarks and demonstrate that NeSyBiCL is effective and leads to superior performance compared to continual learning methods that merely rely on neural architectures to address forgetting.
summary: Continual learning is crucial for creating AI agents that can learn and improve themselves autonomously. A primary challenge in continual learning is to learn new tasks without losing previously learned knowledge. Current continual learning methods primarily focus on enabling a neural network with mechanisms that mitigate forgetting effects. Inspired by the two distinct systems in the human brain, System 1 and System 2, we propose a Neuro-Symbolic Brain-Inspired Continual Learning (NeSyBiCL) framework that incorporates two subsystems to solve continual learning: A neural network model responsible for quickly adapting to the most recent task, together with a symbolic reasoner responsible for retaining previously acquired knowledge from previous tasks. Moreover, we design an integration mechanism between these components to facilitate knowledge transfer from the symbolic reasoner to the neural network. We also introduce two compositional continual learning benchmarks and demonstrate that NeSyBiCL is effective and leads to superior performance compared to continual learning methods that merely rely on neural architectures to address forgetting.
abstract_zh: 持续学习对于创建能够自主学习和自我改进的人工智能代理至关重要。持续学习的主要挑战是在不丢失以前学到的知识的情况下学习新任务。当前的持续学习方法主要侧重于使神经网络具有减轻遗忘影响的机制。受人脑中两个不同系统（系统 1 和系统 2）的启发，我们提出了一个神经符号脑启发持续学习（NeSyBiCL）框架，该框架包含两个子系统来解决持续学习问题：负责快速适应最近任务的神经网络模型，以及负责保留先前任务中获得的知识的符号推理器。此外，我们设计了这些组件之间的集成机制，以促进知识从符号推理器到神经网络的转移。我们还介绍了两个组合持续学习基准，并证明 NeSyBiCL 是有效的，并且与仅依靠神经架构来解决遗忘问题的持续学习方法相比，它具有卓越的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种受大脑启发的神经符号框架NeSyBiCL，通过结合神经网络与符号推理器解决持续学习中的知识遗忘问题。
AI.motivation: 现有持续学习方法依赖神经网络难以有效防止遗忘，而人类大脑的系统1（快速适应）与系统2（知识保留）启发了神经符号方法的结合。
AI.method: 设计NeSyBiCL框架包含神经网络（处理新任务）与符号推理器（存储旧知识），并开发知识转移机制实现两者协同，同时构建两个合成基准进行验证。
AI.result: 实验表明NeSyBiCL在持续学习任务中优于纯神经网络方法，在基准测试中取得更优性能表现。
AI.conclusion: 神经符号方法能有效缓解持续学习遗忘问题，为未来AI自主学习提供新思路与技术框架。
