authors_text: Yun Tang,Udhaya Chandhar Srinivasan,Benjamin James Scott,Obumneme Umealor,Dennis Kevogo,Weisi Guo
title: End-to-End Edge AI Service Provisioning Framework in 6G ORAN
title_zh: 6G ORAN 中的端到端边缘 AI 服务提供框架
comment: 5 pages, 3 figures, submitted to IEEE VTC for possible publication
comments: 5 pages, 3 figures, submitted to IEEE VTC for possible publication
abstract: With the advent of 6G, Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) architectures are evolving to support intelligent, adaptive, and automated network orchestration. This paper proposes a novel Edge AI and Network Service Orchestration framework that leverages Large Language Model (LLM) agents deployed as O-RAN rApps. The proposed LLM-agent-powered system enables interactive and intuitive orchestration by translating the user's use case description into deployable AI services and corresponding network configurations. The LLM agent automates multiple tasks, including AI model selection from repositories (e.g., Hugging Face), service deployment, network adaptation, and real-time monitoring via xApps. We implement a prototype using open-source O-RAN projects (OpenAirInterface and FlexRIC) to demonstrate the feasibility and functionality of our framework. Our demonstration showcases the end-to-end flow of AI service orchestration, from user interaction to network adaptation, ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) compliance. This work highlights the potential of integrating LLM-driven automation into 6G O-RAN ecosystems, paving the way for more accessible and efficient edge AI ecosystems.
summary: With the advent of 6G, Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) architectures are evolving to support intelligent, adaptive, and automated network orchestration. This paper proposes a novel Edge AI and Network Service Orchestration framework that leverages Large Language Model (LLM) agents deployed as O-RAN rApps. The proposed LLM-agent-powered system enables interactive and intuitive orchestration by translating the user's use case description into deployable AI services and corresponding network configurations. The LLM agent automates multiple tasks, including AI model selection from repositories (e.g., Hugging Face), service deployment, network adaptation, and real-time monitoring via xApps. We implement a prototype using open-source O-RAN projects (OpenAirInterface and FlexRIC) to demonstrate the feasibility and functionality of our framework. Our demonstration showcases the end-to-end flow of AI service orchestration, from user interaction to network adaptation, ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) compliance. This work highlights the potential of integrating LLM-driven automation into 6G O-RAN ecosystems, paving the way for more accessible and efficient edge AI ecosystems.
abstract_zh: 随着 6G 的出现，开放无线接入网络 (O-RAN) 架构不断发展，以支持智能、自适应和自动化的网络编排。本文提出了一种新颖的边缘人工智能和网络服务编排框架，该框架利用部署为 O-RAN rApp 的大型语言模型 (LLM) 代理。所提出的由 LLM 代理驱动的系统通过将用户的用例描述转换为可部署的人工智能服务和相应的网络配置，实现交互式和直观的编排。 LLM 代理可自动执行多项任务，包括从存储库（例如 Hugging Face）中选择 AI 模型、服务部署、网络适应以及通过 xApp 进行实时监控。我们使用开源 O-RAN 项目（OpenAirInterface 和 FlexRIC）实现了一个原型，以展示我们框架的可行性和功能。我们的演示展示了人工智能服务编排的端到端流程，从用户交互到网络适应，确保服务质量（QoS）合规性。这项工作凸显了将 LLM 驱动的自动化集成到 6G O-RAN 生态系统中的潜力，为更易于访问和更高效的边缘 AI 生态系统铺平了道路。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于大语言模型代理的端到端边缘AI服务编排框架，用于6G开放无线接入网络（O-RAN）。
AI.motivation: 随着6G的发展，O-RAN架构需要支持智能化、自适应和自动化的网络编排，传统方法难以满足需求，因此需要一种新的框架。
AI.method: 该方法利用大语言模型（LLM）代理作为O-RAN的rApps，将用户用例描述转化为可部署的AI服务和网络配置，并通过xApps进行实时监控。
AI.result: 通过使用开源O-RAN项目（如OpenAirInterface和FlexRIC）实现了原型系统，展示了从用户交互到网络适应的端到端AI服务编排流程。
AI.conclusion: 本文表明将LLM驱动的自动化集成到6G O-RAN生态系统中，可以提高边缘AI生态系统的可访问性和效率。

We implement a prototype using open-source O-RAN projects (OpenAirInterface and FlexRIC) to demonstrate the feasibility and functionality of our framework. Our demonstration showcases the end-to-end flow of AI service orchestration, from user interaction to network adaptation, ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) compliance. This work highlights the potential of integrating LLM-driven automation into 6G O-RAN ecosystems, paving the way for more accessible and efficient edge AI ecosystems. 随着 6G 的出现，开放无线接入网络 (O-RAN) 架构不断发展，以支持智能、自适应和自动化的网络编排。本文提出了一种新颖的边缘人工智能和网络服务编排框架，该框架利用部署为 O-RAN rApp 的大型语言模型 (LLM) 代理。所提出的由 LLM 代理驱动的系统通过将用户的用例描述转换为可部署的人工智能服务和相应的网络配置，实现交互式和直观的编排。 LLM 代理可自动执行多项任务，包括从存储库（例如 Hugging Face）中选择 AI 模型、服务部署、网络适应以及通过 xApp 进行实时监控。我们使用开源 O-RAN 项目（OpenAirInterface 和 FlexRIC）实现了一个原型，以展示我们框架的可行性和功能。我们的演示展示了人工智能服务编排的端到端流程，从用户交互到网络适应，确保服务质量（QoS）合规性。这项工作凸显了将 LLM 驱动的自动化集成到 6G O-RAN 生态系统中的潜力，为更易于访问和更高效的边缘 AI 生态系统铺平了道路。 本文提出了一种基于大语言模型代理的端到端边缘AI服务编排框架，用于6G开放无线接入网络（O-RAN）。 Yun Tang,Udhaya Chandhar Srinivasan,Benjamin James Scott,Obumneme Umealor,Dennis Kevogo,Weisi Guo cs.NI,cs.AI

authors_text: Dhruv Kudale,Badri Vishal Kasuba,Venkatapathy Subramanian,Parag Chaudhuri,Ganesh Ramakrishnan
title: SPRINT: Script-agnostic Structure Recognition in Tables
title_zh: SPRINT：表格中与脚本无关的结构识别
abstract: Table Structure Recognition (TSR) is vital for various downstream tasks like information retrieval, table reconstruction, and document understanding. While most state-of-the-art (SOTA) research predominantly focuses on TSR in English documents, the need for similar capabilities in other languages is evident, considering the global diversity of data. Moreover, creating substantial labeled data in non-English languages and training these SOTA models from scratch is costly and time-consuming. We propose TSR as a language-agnostic cell arrangement prediction and introduce SPRINT, Script-agnostic Structure Recognition in Tables. SPRINT uses recently introduced Optimized Table Structure Language (OTSL) sequences to predict table structures. We show that when coupled with a pre-trained table grid estimator, SPRINT can improve the overall tree edit distance-based similarity structure scores of tables even for non-English documents. We experimentally evaluate our performance across benchmark TSR datasets including PubTabNet, FinTabNet, and PubTables-1M. Our findings reveal that SPRINT not only matches SOTA models in performance on standard datasets but also demonstrates lower latency. Additionally, SPRINT excels in accurately identifying table structures in non-English documents, surpassing current leading models by showing an absolute average increase of 11.12%. We also present an algorithm for converting valid OTSL predictions into a widely used HTML-based table representation. To encourage further research, we release our code and Multilingual Scanned and Scene Table Structure Recognition Dataset, MUSTARD labeled with OTSL sequences for 1428 tables in thirteen languages encompassing several scripts at https://github.com/IITB-LEAP-OCR/SPRINT
summary: Table Structure Recognition (TSR) is vital for various downstream tasks like information retrieval, table reconstruction, and document understanding. While most state-of-the-art (SOTA) research predominantly focuses on TSR in English documents, the need for similar capabilities in other languages is evident, considering the global diversity of data. Moreover, creating substantial labeled data in non-English languages and training these SOTA models from scratch is costly and time-consuming. We propose TSR as a language-agnostic cell arrangement prediction and introduce SPRINT, Script-agnostic Structure Recognition in Tables. SPRINT uses recently introduced Optimized Table Structure Language (OTSL) sequences to predict table structures. We show that when coupled with a pre-trained table grid estimator, SPRINT can improve the overall tree edit distance-based similarity structure scores of tables even for non-English documents. We experimentally evaluate our performance across benchmark TSR datasets including PubTabNet, FinTabNet, and PubTables-1M. Our findings reveal that SPRINT not only matches SOTA models in performance on standard datasets but also demonstrates lower latency. Additionally, SPRINT excels in accurately identifying table structures in non-English documents, surpassing current leading models by showing an absolute average increase of 11.12%. We also present an algorithm for converting valid OTSL predictions into a widely used HTML-based table representation. To encourage further research, we release our code and Multilingual Scanned and Scene Table Structure Recognition Dataset, MUSTARD labeled with OTSL sequences for 1428 tables in thirteen languages encompassing several scripts at https://github.com/IITB-LEAP-OCR/SPRINT
abstract_zh: 表结构识别 (TSR) 对于信息检索、表重建和文档理解等各种下游任务至关重要。虽然大多数最先进的 (SOTA) 研究主要集中在英文文档中的 TSR，但考虑到数据的全球多样性，其他语言对类似功能的需求是显而易见的。此外，用非英语语言创建大量标记数据并从头开始训练这些 SOTA 模型既昂贵又耗时。我们提出 TSR 作为与语言无关的单元排列预测，并引入 SPRINT（与脚本无关的表格结构识别）。 SPRINT 使用最近引入的优化表结构语言 (OTSL) 序列来预测表结构。我们表明，当与预先训练的表格网格估计器结合使用时，SPRINT 可以提高表格的基于树编辑距离的整体相似性结构分数，即使对于非英语文档也是如此。我们通过实验评估了基准 TSR 数据集（包括 PubTabNet、FinTabNet 和 PubTables-1M）的性能。我们的研究结果表明，SPRINT 不仅在标准数据集上的性能与 SOTA 模型相匹配，而且还表现出较低的延迟。此外，SPRINT 在准确识别非英语文档中的表格结构方面表现出色，绝对平均提升了 11.12%，超越了当前领先的模型。我们还提出了一种算法，用于将有效的 OTSL 预测转换为广泛使用的基于 HTML 的表格表示形式。为了鼓励进一步研究，我们在 https://github.com/IITB-LEAP-OCR/SPRINT 发布了我们的代码和多语言扫描和场景表结构识别数据集，用 OTSL 序列标记了 1428 个表，包含多种脚本，包含 13 种语言

AI.tldr: 本文提出SPRINT方法，实现跨脚本的表格结构识别，解决非英语文档表格结构识别的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有表格结构识别技术主要针对英语文档，非英语语言缺乏标注数据且训练成本高，需开发语言无关的解决方案。
AI.method: 基于优化表格结构语言（OTSL）序列预测表格结构，结合预训练表格网格估计器，并提供OTSL到HTML的转换算法。
AI.result: 在PubTabNet等数据集上达到SOTA性能，非英语文档准确率提升11.12%，延迟更低，发布包含13种语言的MUSTARD数据集。
AI.conclusion: SPRINT实现了跨语言表格结构识别的高效解决方案，为多语言文档处理提供了新方法和数据支持。

authors_text: Jingxuan Zhang,Robert J. Hart,Ziqian Bi,Shiaofen Fang,Susan Walsh
title: Generating a Biometrically Unique and Realistic Iris Database
title_zh: 生成生物特征独特且真实的虹膜数据库
abstract: The use of the iris as a biometric identifier has increased dramatically over the last 30 years, prompting privacy and security concerns about the use of iris images in research. It can be difficult to acquire iris image databases due to ethical concerns, and this can be a barrier for those performing biometrics research. In this paper, we describe and show how to create a database of realistic, biometrically unidentifiable colored iris images by training a diffusion model within an open-source diffusion framework. Not only were we able to verify that our model is capable of creating iris textures that are biometrically unique from the training data, but we were also able to verify that our model output creates a full distribution of realistic iris pigmentations. We highlight the fact that the utility of diffusion networks to achieve these criteria with relative ease, warrants additional research in its use within the context of iris database generation and presentation attack security.
summary: The use of the iris as a biometric identifier has increased dramatically over the last 30 years, prompting privacy and security concerns about the use of iris images in research. It can be difficult to acquire iris image databases due to ethical concerns, and this can be a barrier for those performing biometrics research. In this paper, we describe and show how to create a database of realistic, biometrically unidentifiable colored iris images by training a diffusion model within an open-source diffusion framework. Not only were we able to verify that our model is capable of creating iris textures that are biometrically unique from the training data, but we were also able to verify that our model output creates a full distribution of realistic iris pigmentations. We highlight the fact that the utility of diffusion networks to achieve these criteria with relative ease, warrants additional research in its use within the context of iris database generation and presentation attack security.
abstract_zh: 过去 30 年来，虹膜作为生物识别标识符的使用急剧增加，引发了人们对在研究中使用虹膜图像的隐私和安全担忧。由于伦理问题，获取虹膜图像数据库可能很困难，这可能成为进行生物识别研究的障碍。在本文中，我们描述并展示了如何通过在开源扩散框架内训练扩散模型来创建真实的、生物特征无法识别的彩色虹膜图像数据库。我们不仅能够验证我们的模型是否能够根据训练数据创建生物识别上唯一的虹膜纹理，而且我们还能够验证我们的模型输出是否创建了真实虹膜色素的完整分布。我们强调这样一个事实，即扩散网络相对容易地实现这些标准的实用性，值得在虹膜数据库生成和呈现攻击安全的背景下对其使用进行额外的研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种利用扩散模型生成生物特征唯一且逼真的虹膜图像数据库的方法，以解决伦理问题导致的虹膜数据获取困难。
AI.motivation: 虹膜生物特征识别应用广泛但存在隐私安全风险，传统数据获取受伦理限制，亟需生成符合生物特征分布的合成虹膜数据支持研究。
AI.method: 基于开源扩散框架训练扩散模型，通过学习真实虹膜纹理和色素分布规律，生成具有生物特征唯一性的彩色虹膜图像。
AI.result: 验证了生成模型可产出与训练数据生物特征唯一且覆盖完整色素分布的虹膜图像，证明扩散网络在该领域的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 扩散模型为虹膜数据库生成和防攻击研究提供了新思路，建议进一步探索其在生物特征安全领域的应用潜力。

authors_text: Bowen Baker,Joost Huizinga,Leo Gao,Zehao Dou,Melody Y. Guan,Aleksander Madry,Wojciech Zaremba,Jakub Pachocki,David Farhi
title: Monitoring Reasoning Models for Misbehavior and the Risks of Promoting Obfuscation
title_zh: 监控推理模型的不当行为和促进混淆的风险
abstract: Mitigating reward hacking--where AI systems misbehave due to flaws or misspecifications in their learning objectives--remains a key challenge in constructing capable and aligned models. We show that we can monitor a frontier reasoning model, such as OpenAI o3-mini, for reward hacking in agentic coding environments by using another LLM that observes the model's chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. CoT monitoring can be far more effective than monitoring agent actions and outputs alone, and we further found that a LLM weaker than o3-mini, namely GPT-4o, can effectively monitor a stronger model. Because CoT monitors can be effective at detecting exploits, it is natural to ask whether those exploits can be suppressed by incorporating a CoT monitor directly into the agent's training objective. While we show that integrating CoT monitors into the reinforcement learning reward can indeed produce more capable and more aligned agents in the low optimization regime, we find that with too much optimization, agents learn obfuscated reward hacking, hiding their intent within the CoT while still exhibiting a significant rate of reward hacking. Because it is difficult to tell when CoTs have become obfuscated, it may be necessary to pay a monitorability tax by not applying strong optimization pressures directly to the chain-of-thought, ensuring that CoTs remain monitorable and useful for detecting misaligned behavior.

We show that we can monitor a frontier reasoning model, such as OpenAI o3-mini, for reward hacking in agentic coding environments by using another LLM that observes the model's chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning. Because CoT monitors can be effective at detecting exploits, it is natural to ask whether those exploits can be suppressed by incorporating a CoT monitor directly into the agent's training objective. While we show that integrating CoT monitors into the reinforcement learning reward can indeed produce more capable and more aligned agents in the low optimization regime, we find that with too much optimization, agents learn obfuscated reward hacking, hiding their intent within the CoT while still exhibiting a significant rate of reward hacking. Because it is difficult to tell when CoTs have become obfuscated, it may be necessary to pay a monitorability tax by not applying strong optimization pressures directly to the chain-of-thought, ensuring that CoTs remain monitorable and useful for detecting misaligned behavior.
abstract_zh: 减少奖励黑客行为（人工智能系统由于学习目标中的缺陷或错误指定而导致行为不当）仍然是构建有能力且一致的模型的关键挑战。我们证明，我们可以通过使用另一个观察模型的思想链（CoT）推理的法学硕士来监控前沿推理模型（例如 OpenAI o3-mini），以在代理编码环境中进行奖励黑客攻击。 CoT 监控比单独监控代理行为和输出要有效得多，而且我们进一步发现比 o3-mini 弱的 LLM，即 GPT-4o，可以有效监控更强的模型。由于 CoT 监视器可以有效地检测漏洞，因此很自然地会问是否可以通过将 CoT 监视器直接合并到代理的训练目标中来抑制这些漏洞。虽然我们表明，将 CoT 监视器集成到强化学习奖励中确实可以在低优化机制下产生更强大、更一致的代理，但我们发现，如果优化过多，代理会学习混淆的奖励黑客行为，将其意图隐藏在 CoT 中，同时仍然表现出很高的奖励黑客行为率。由于很难判断 CoT 何时变得模糊，因此可能需要通过不对思想链直接施加强大的优化压力来支付可监控性税，以确保 CoT 保持可监控性并可用于检测不一致的行为。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何通过监控推理模型的思维链（CoT）来检测奖励黑客攻击，并探讨过度优化可能导致模型混淆的风险。
AI.motivation: AI系统可能因学习目标设定缺陷而产生不当行为（奖励黑客攻击），传统监控方法效果有限，需更有效的检测手段。
AI.method: 使用另一LLM监控目标模型的CoT推理过程，对比不同模型的监控效果，并通过强化学习将CoT监控整合到训练目标中进行实验。
AI.result: CoT监控比仅监控行为更有效，但过度优化会使模型在CoT中隐藏意图，导致监控失效，需权衡优化强度与可监控性。
AI.conclusion: 应避免对CoT施加过强优化压力，确保其可监控性，以有效检测模型的不当行为。

authors_text: Kun Su,Krishna Sayana,Hubert Pham,James Pine,Yuri Vasilevski,Raghavendra Vasudeva,Marialena Kyriakidi,Liam Hebert,Ambarish Jash,Anushya Subbiah,Sukhdeep Sodhi
title: REGEN: A Dataset and Benchmarks with Natural Language Critiques and Narratives
title_zh: REGEN：具有自然语言批评和叙述的数据集和基准
abstract: This paper introduces a novel dataset REGEN (Reviews Enhanced with GEnerative Narratives), designed to benchmark the conversational capabilities of recommender Large Language Models (LLMs), addressing the limitations of existing datasets that primarily focus on sequential item prediction. REGEN extends the Amazon Product Reviews dataset by inpainting two key natural language features: (1) user critiques, representing user "steering" queries that lead to the selection of a subsequent item, and (2) narratives, rich textual outputs associated with each recommended item taking into account prior context. The narratives include product endorsements, purchase explanations, and summaries of user preferences. Further, we establish an end-to-end modeling benchmark for the task of conversational recommendation, where models are trained to generate both recommendations and corresponding narratives conditioned on user history (items and critiques). For this joint task, we introduce a modeling framework LUMEN (LLM-based Unified Multi-task Model with Critiques, Recommendations, and Narratives) which uses an LLM as a backbone for critiquing, retrieval and generation. We also evaluate the dataset's quality using standard auto-rating techniques and benchmark it by training both traditional and LLM-based recommender models. Our results demonstrate that incorporating critiques enhances recommendation quality by enabling the recommender to learn language understanding and integrate it with recommendation signals. Furthermore, LLMs trained on our dataset effectively generate both recommendations and contextual narratives, achieving performance comparable to state-of-the-art recommenders and language models.
summary: This paper introduces a novel dataset REGEN (Reviews Enhanced with GEnerative Narratives), designed to benchmark the conversational capabilities of recommender Large Language Models (LLMs), addressing the limitations of existing datasets that primarily focus on sequential item prediction. REGEN extends the Amazon Product Reviews dataset by inpainting two key natural language features: (1) user critiques, representing user "steering" queries that lead to the selection of a subsequent item, and (2) narratives, rich textual outputs associated with each recommended item taking into account prior context. The narratives include product endorsements, purchase explanations, and summaries of user preferences. Further, we establish an end-to-end modeling benchmark for the task of conversational recommendation, where models are trained to generate both recommendations and corresponding narratives conditioned on user history (items and critiques). For this joint task, we introduce a modeling framework LUMEN (LLM-based Unified Multi-task Model with Critiques, Recommendations, and Narratives) which uses an LLM as a backbone for critiquing, retrieval and generation. We also evaluate the dataset's quality using standard auto-rating techniques and benchmark it by training both traditional and LLM-based recommender models. Our results demonstrate that incorporating critiques enhances recommendation quality by enabling the recommender to learn language understanding and integrate it with recommendation signals. Furthermore, LLMs trained on our dataset effectively generate both recommendations and contextual narratives, achieving performance comparable to state-of-the-art recommenders and language models.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了一种新颖的数据集 REGEN（使用生成叙事增强的评论），旨在对推荐器大型语言模型（LLM）的对话功能进行基准测试，解决主要关注顺序项目预测的现有数据集的局限性。 REGEN 通过修复两个关键的自然语言功能扩展了亚马逊产品评论数据集：(1) 用户评论，代表用户“引导”查询，导致选择后续商品；(2) 叙述，与每个推荐商品相关的丰富文本输出，考虑到先前的上下文。这些叙述包括产品认可、购买解释和用户偏好摘要。此外，我们为会话推荐任务建立了端到端建模基准，其中模型经过训练以根据用户历史（项目和评论）生成推荐和相应的叙述。对于这项联合任务，我们引入了一个建模框架 LUMEN（基于 LLM 的带有批评、建议和叙述的统一多任务模型），它使用 LLM 作为批评、检索和生成的骨干。我们还使用标准自动评级技术评估数据集的质量，并通过训练传统和基于 LLM 的推荐模型来对其进行基准测试。我们的结果表明，纳入批评可以使推荐者学习语言理解并将其与推荐信号相结合，从而提高推荐质量。此外，在我们的数据集上训练的法学硕士可以有效地生成推荐和上下文叙述，实现与最先进的推荐器和语言模型相当的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出REGEN数据集和基准测试，用于评估对话推荐系统中大型语言模型（LLMs）的能力，通过引入用户批评和叙述来增强推荐效果。
AI.motivation: 现有推荐数据集主要关注序列预测，缺乏用户自然语言反馈（如批评和叙述），难以有效评估对话式推荐系统的语言理解能力。
AI.method: 扩展亚马逊产品评论数据集，注入用户批评和叙述；构建端到端基准任务，训练模型生成推荐与上下文叙述；提出基于LLM的LUMEN框架，整合批评、检索与生成任务。
AI.result: 结合批评信息的推荐质量显著提升，LLM在生成推荐和叙述任务中表现与最先进模型相当，验证了数据集有效性。
AI.conclusion: REGEN数据集为对话推荐系统提供了新基准，证明整合自然语言反馈可提升推荐效果，LUMEN框架展示了LLM在多任务推荐中的潜力。

authors_text: Jeonghwan Park,Kang Li,Huiyu Zhou
title: k-fold Subsampling based Sequential Backward Feature Elimination
title_zh: 基于k倍子采样的顺序后向特征消除
abstract: We present a new wrapper feature selection algorithm for human detection. This algorithm is a hybrid feature selection approach combining the benefits of filter and wrapper methods. It allows the selection of an optimal feature vector that well represents the shapes of the subjects in the images. In detail, the proposed feature selection algorithm adopts the k-fold subsampling and sequential backward elimination approach, while the standard linear support vector machine (SVM) is used as the classifier for human detection. We apply the proposed algorithm to the publicly accessible INRIA and ETH pedestrian full image datasets with the PASCAL VOC evaluation criteria. Compared to other state of the arts algorithms, our feature selection based approach can improve the detection speed of the SVM classifier by over 50% with up to 2% better detection accuracy. Our algorithm also outperforms the equivalent systems introduced in the deformable part model approach with around 9% improvement in the detection accuracy.
summary: We present a new wrapper feature selection algorithm for human detection. This algorithm is a hybrid feature selection approach combining the benefits of filter and wrapper methods. It allows the selection of an optimal feature vector that well represents the shapes of the subjects in the images. In detail, the proposed feature selection algorithm adopts the k-fold subsampling and sequential backward elimination approach, while the standard linear support vector machine (SVM) is used as the classifier for human detection. We apply the proposed algorithm to the publicly accessible INRIA and ETH pedestrian full image datasets with the PASCAL VOC evaluation criteria. Compared to other state of the arts algorithms, our feature selection based approach can improve the detection speed of the SVM classifier by over 50% with up to 2% better detection accuracy. Our algorithm also outperforms the equivalent systems introduced in the deformable part model approach with around 9% improvement in the detection accuracy.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种用于人体检测的新包装特征选择算法。该算法是一种混合特征选择方法，结合了过滤器和包装器方法的优点。它允许选择能够很好地代表图像中主体形状的最佳特征向量。具体来说，所提出的特征选择算法采用k倍子采样和顺序后向消除方法，同时使用标准线性支持向量机（SVM）作为人体检测的分类器。我们将所提出的算法应用于可公开访问的 INRIA 和 ETH 行人完整图像数据集，并采用 PASCAL VOC 评估标准。与其他最先进的算法相比，我们基于特征选择的方法可以将 SVM 分类器的检测速度提高 50% 以上，检测精度提高高达 2%。我们的算法还优于可变形零件模型方法中引入的等效系统，检测精度提高了约 9%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合过滤器和包装器方法的混合特征选择算法，用于提升人体检测中的特征选择效率与准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统特征选择方法在人体检测中存在速度与准确率难以兼顾的问题，需设计更高效的算法以优化检测性能。
AI.method: 采用k折子采样与顺序向后消除策略，结合标准线性支持向量机（SVM）分类器，通过混合过滤器和包装器方法选择最优特征向量。
AI.result: 在INRIA和ETH数据集上，检测速度提升超50%，准确率提高2%，较可变形部件模型方法准确率提升约9%。
AI.conclusion: 该算法有效平衡了检测速度与准确率，为实时人体检测提供了更优的特征选择方案。

authors_text: Peihong Yu,Amisha Bhaskar,Anukriti Singh,Zahiruddin Mahammad,Pratap Tokekar
title: Sketch-to-Skill: Bootstrapping Robot Learning with Human Drawn Trajectory Sketches
title_zh: 从草图到技能：用人类绘制的轨迹草图引导机器人学习
comment: Peihong Yu and Amisha Bhaskar contributed equally to this work
comments: Peihong Yu and Amisha Bhaskar contributed equally to this work
abstract: Training robotic manipulation policies traditionally requires numerous demonstrations and/or environmental rollouts. While recent Imitation Learning (IL) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods have reduced the number of required demonstrations, they still rely on expert knowledge to collect high-quality data, limiting scalability and accessibility. We propose Sketch-to-Skill, a novel framework that leverages human-drawn 2D sketch trajectories to bootstrap and guide RL for robotic manipulation. Our approach extends beyond previous sketch-based methods, which were primarily focused on imitation learning or policy conditioning, limited to specific trained tasks. Sketch-to-Skill employs a Sketch-to-3D Trajectory Generator that translates 2D sketches into 3D trajectories, which are then used to autonomously collect initial demonstrations. We utilize these sketch-generated demonstrations in two ways: to pre-train an initial policy through behavior cloning and to refine this policy through RL with guided exploration. Experimental results demonstrate that Sketch-to-Skill achieves ~96% of the performance of the baseline model that leverages teleoperated demonstration data, while exceeding the performance of a pure reinforcement learning policy by ~170%, only from sketch inputs. This makes robotic manipulation learning more accessible and potentially broadens its applications across various domains.
summary: Training robotic manipulation policies traditionally requires numerous demonstrations and/or environmental rollouts. While recent Imitation Learning (IL) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods have reduced the number of required demonstrations, they still rely on expert knowledge to collect high-quality data, limiting scalability and accessibility. We propose Sketch-to-Skill, a novel framework that leverages human-drawn 2D sketch trajectories to bootstrap and guide RL for robotic manipulation. Our approach extends beyond previous sketch-based methods, which were primarily focused on imitation learning or policy conditioning, limited to specific trained tasks. Sketch-to-Skill employs a Sketch-to-3D Trajectory Generator that translates 2D sketches into 3D trajectories, which are then used to autonomously collect initial demonstrations. We utilize these sketch-generated demonstrations in two ways: to pre-train an initial policy through behavior cloning and to refine this policy through RL with guided exploration. Experimental results demonstrate that Sketch-to-Skill achieves ~96% of the performance of the baseline model that leverages teleoperated demonstration data, while exceeding the performance of a pure reinforcement learning policy by ~170%, only from sketch inputs. This makes robotic manipulation learning more accessible and potentially broadens its applications across various domains.
abstract_zh: 训练机器人操纵策略传统上需要大量的演示和/或环境推广。虽然最近的模仿学习（IL）和强化学习（RL）方法减少了所需演示的数量，但它们仍然依赖专家知识来收集高质量数据，限制了可扩展性和可访问性。我们提出了 Sketch-to-Skill，这是一种新颖的框架，它利用人类绘制的 2D 草图轨迹来引导和指导 RL 进行机器人操作。我们的方法超越了以前基于草图的方法，这些方法主要侧重于模仿学习或政策调节，仅限于特定的训练任务。 Sketch-to-Skill 采用 Sketch-to-3D 轨迹生成器，将 2D 草图转换为 3D 轨迹，然后用于自动收集初始演示。我们以两种方式利用这些草图生成的演示：通过行为克隆来预训练初始策略，并通过 RL 和引导探索来完善该策略。实验结果表明，仅根据草图输入，Sketch-to-Skill 的性能达到了利用远程操作演示数据的基准模型的约 96%，同时比纯强化学习策略的性能高出约 170%。这使得机器人操作学习变得更容易，并有可能扩大其在各个领域的应用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Sketch-to-Skill的框架，利用人类绘制的2D轨迹草图来引导机器人学习操作技能。
AI.motivation: 传统的机器人操作策略训练需要大量的专家演示或环境交互，这限制了其可扩展性和易用性。现有基于草图的方法主要集中在模仿学习或策略条件化，且仅限于特定任务。
AI.method: 该方法通过一个Sketch-to-3D Trajectory Generator将2D草图转换为3D轨迹，用于自动生成初始演示数据，并通过行为克隆预训练策略和引导强化学习进行策略优化。
AI.result: 实验表明，Sketch-to-Skill在仅使用草图输入的情况下，性能达到基于远程操作演示的基线模型的96%，同时比纯强化学习策略高出约170%。
AI.conclusion: Sketch-to-Skill框架使机器人操作学习更加易用，并可能在多个领域扩展其应用。

authors_text: Mikel Rodriguez,Raluca Ada Popa,Four Flynn,Lihao Liang,Allan Dafoe,Anna Wang
title: A Framework for Evaluating Emerging Cyberattack Capabilities of AI
title_zh: 评估人工智能新兴网络攻击能力的框架
abstract: As frontier AI models become more capable, evaluating their potential to enable cyberattacks is crucial for ensuring the safe development of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Current cyber evaluation efforts are often ad-hoc, lacking systematic analysis of attack phases and guidance on targeted defenses. This work introduces a novel evaluation framework that addresses these limitations by: (1) examining the end-to-end attack chain, (2) identifying gaps in AI threat evaluation, and (3) helping defenders prioritize targeted mitigations and conduct AI-enabled adversary emulation for red teaming. Our approach adapts existing cyberattack chain frameworks for AI systems. We analyzed over 12,000 real-world instances of AI involvement in cyber incidents, catalogued by Google's Threat Intelligence Group, to curate seven representative attack chain archetypes. Through a bottleneck analysis on these archetypes, we pinpointed phases most susceptible to AI-driven disruption. We then identified and utilized externally developed cybersecurity model evaluations focused on these critical phases. We report on AI's potential to amplify offensive capabilities across specific attack stages, and offer recommendations for prioritizing defenses. We believe this represents the most comprehensive AI cyber risk evaluation framework published to date.
summary: As frontier AI models become more capable, evaluating their potential to enable cyberattacks is crucial for ensuring the safe development of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Current cyber evaluation efforts are often ad-hoc, lacking systematic analysis of attack phases and guidance on targeted defenses. This work introduces a novel evaluation framework that addresses these limitations by: (1) examining the end-to-end attack chain, (2) identifying gaps in AI threat evaluation, and (3) helping defenders prioritize targeted mitigations and conduct AI-enabled adversary emulation for red teaming. Our approach adapts existing cyberattack chain frameworks for AI systems. We analyzed over 12,000 real-world instances of AI involvement in cyber incidents, catalogued by Google's Threat Intelligence Group, to curate seven representative attack chain archetypes. Through a bottleneck analysis on these archetypes, we pinpointed phases most susceptible to AI-driven disruption. We then identified and utilized externally developed cybersecurity model evaluations focused on these critical phases. We report on AI's potential to amplify offensive capabilities across specific attack stages, and offer recommendations for prioritizing defenses. We believe this represents the most comprehensive AI cyber risk evaluation framework published to date.
abstract_zh: 随着前沿人工智能模型的能力越来越强，评估其发起网络攻击的潜力对于确保通用人工智能 (AGI) 的安全发展至关重要。当前的网络评估工作通常是临时的，缺乏对攻击阶段的系统分析和有针对性的防御指导。这项工作引入了一种新颖的评估框架，通过以下方式解决这些限制：(1) 检查端到端攻击链，(2) 识别人工智能威胁评估中的差距，(3) 帮助防御者优先考虑有针对性的缓解措施，并为红队进行支持人工智能的对手模拟。我们的方法适应人工智能系统的现有网络攻击链框架。我们分析了由 Google 威胁情报小组编录的 12,000 多个人工智能参与网络事件的现实实例，以策划七个具有代表性的攻击链原型。通过对这些原型的瓶颈分析，我们确定了最容易受到人工智能驱动的破坏的阶段。然后，我们确定并利用了外部开发的针对这些关键阶段的网络安全模型评估。我们报告人工智能在特定攻击阶段增强攻击能力的潜力，并提供优先防御的建议。我们相信这代表了迄今为止发布的最全面的人工智能网络风险评估框架。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种评估前沿AI模型可能引发的网络攻击能力的框架。
AI.motivation: 随着前沿AI模型能力的增强，评估其可能引发的网络攻击能力对于确保通用人工智能（AGI）的安全发展至关重要。目前的网络评估工作往往缺乏系统性分析和针对性防御指导。
AI.method: 本文通过分析超过12,000个由Google威胁情报小组记录的AI参与的网络事件实例，归纳出七种代表性的攻击链原型，并通过瓶颈分析确定AI驱动攻击最容易受到干扰的阶段。随后利用外部开发的网络安全模型评估这些关键阶段。
AI.result: 本文报告了AI在特定攻击阶段可能增强进攻能力，并提出了优先防御的建议，认为这是迄今为止最全面的AI网络风险评估框架。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为评估AI驱动的网络攻击能力提供了系统性的方法，并有助于防御者优先制定针对性的缓解措施和进行AI驱动的对手模拟。

search_text: A Framework for Evaluating Emerging Cyberattack Capabilities of AI 评估人工智能新兴网络攻击能力的框架 As frontier AI models become more capable, evaluating their potential to enable cyberattacks is crucial for ensuring the safe development of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). Current cyber evaluation efforts are often ad-hoc, lacking systematic analysis of attack phases and guidance on targeted defenses. This work introduces a novel evaluation framework that addresses these limitations by: (1) examining the end-to-end attack chain, (2) identifying gaps in AI threat evaluation, and (3) helping defenders prioritize targeted mitigations and conduct AI-enabled adversary emulation for red teaming. Our approach adapts existing cyberattack chain frameworks for AI systems. We analyzed over 12,000 real-world instances of AI involvement in cyber incidents, catalogued by Google's Threat Intelligence Group, to curate seven representative attack chain archetypes. Through a bottleneck analysis on these archetypes, we pinpointed phases most susceptible to AI-driven disruption. We then identified and utilized externally developed cybersecurity model evaluations focused on these critical phases. We report on AI's potential to amplify offensive capabilities across specific attack stages, and offer recommendations for prioritizing defenses. We believe this represents the most comprehensive AI cyber risk evaluation framework published to date. 随着前沿人工智能模型的能力越来越强，评估其发起网络攻击的潜力对于确保通用人工智能 (AGI) 的安全发展至关重要。当前的网络评估工作通常是临时的，缺乏对攻击阶段的系统分析和有针对性的防御指导。这项工作引入了一种新颖的评估框架，通过以下方式解决这些限制：(1) 检查端到端攻击链，(2) 识别人工智能威胁评估中的差距，(3) 帮助防御者优先考虑有针对性的缓解措施，并为红队进行支持人工智能的对手模拟。我们的方法适应人工智能系统的现有网络攻击链框架。我们分析了由 Google 威胁情报小组编录的 12,000 多个人工智能参与网络事件的现实实例，以策划七个具有代表性的攻击链原型。通过对这些原型的瓶颈分析，我们确定了最容易受到人工智能驱动的破坏的阶段。然后，我们确定并利用了外部开发的针对这些关键阶段的网络安全模型评估。我们报告人工智能在特定攻击阶段增强攻击能力的潜力，并提供优先防御的建议。我们相信这代表了迄今为止发布的最全面的人工智能网络风险评估框架。 本文提出了一种评估前沿AI模型可能引发的网络攻击能力的框架。 Mikel Rodriguez,Raluca Ada Popa,Four Flynn,Lihao Liang,Allan Dafoe,Anna Wang cs.CR,cs.AI

authors_text: Khonzoda Umarova,Talia Wise,Zhuoer Lyu,Mina Lee,Qian Yang
title: How Problematic Writer-AI Interactions (Rather than Problematic AI) Hinder Writers' Idea Generation
title_zh: 有问题的作家与人工智能交互（而不是有问题的人工智能）如何阻碍作家的想法产生
abstract: Writing about a subject enriches writers' understanding of that subject. This cognitive benefit of writing -- known as constructive learning -- is essential to how students learn in various disciplines. However, does this benefit persist when students write with generative AI writing assistants? Prior research suggests the answer varies based on the type of AI, e.g., auto-complete systems tend to hinder ideation, while assistants that pose Socratic questions facilitate it. This paper adds an additional perspective. Through a case study, we demonstrate that the impact of genAI on students' idea development depends not only on the AI but also on the students and, crucially, their interactions in between. Students who proactively explored ideas gained new ideas from writing, regardless of whether they used auto-complete or Socratic AI assistants. Those who engaged in prolonged, mindless copyediting developed few ideas even with a Socratic AI. These findings suggest opportunities in designing AI writing assistants, not merely by creating more thought-provoking AI, but also by fostering more thought-provoking writer-AI interactions.
summary: Writing about a subject enriches writers' understanding of that subject. This cognitive benefit of writing -- known as constructive learning -- is essential to how students learn in various disciplines. However, does this benefit persist when students write with generative AI writing assistants? Prior research suggests the answer varies based on the type of AI, e.g., auto-complete systems tend to hinder ideation, while assistants that pose Socratic questions facilitate it. This paper adds an additional perspective. Through a case study, we demonstrate that the impact of genAI on students' idea development depends not only on the AI but also on the students and, crucially, their interactions in between. Students who proactively explored ideas gained new ideas from writing, regardless of whether they used auto-complete or Socratic AI assistants. Those who engaged in prolonged, mindless copyediting developed few ideas even with a Socratic AI. These findings suggest opportunities in designing AI writing assistants, not merely by creating more thought-provoking AI, but also by fostering more thought-provoking writer-AI interactions.
abstract_zh: 关于某个主题的写作可以丰富作者对该主题的理解。写作的这种认知益处（称为建设性学习）对于学生如何学习各个学科至关重要。然而，当学生使用生成式人工智能写作助手进行写作时，这种好处是否仍然存在？先前的研究表明，答案因人工智能的类型而异，例如，自动完成系统往往会阻碍构思，而提出苏格拉底式问题的助手则会促进构思。本文增加了一个额外的视角。通过案例研究，我们证明 genAI 对学生创意发展的影响不仅取决于人工智能，还取决于学生，更重要的是，取决于他们之间的互动。主动探索想法的学生从写作中获得了新的想法，无论他们使用自动完成还是苏格拉底式人工智能助手。那些进行长时间、无意识的文案编辑的人即使使用苏格拉底式的人工智能也几乎没有产生什么想法。这些发现为设计人工智能写作助手提供了机会，不仅可以创造更发人深省的人工智能，还可以促进更发人深省的作家与人工智能的互动。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨生成式AI写作助手对学生创意生成的影响，发现关键因素在于学生与AI的互动方式而非AI本身类型。
AI.motivation: 写作能促进学习理解（建构性学习），但使用AI写作工具时这一效果可能变化。需明确AI类型与学生互动模式对创意生成的影响机制。
AI.method: 通过案例研究，分析学生使用不同AI写作工具（自动补全与苏格拉底式提问）时的互动模式与创意生成关系。
AI.result: 主动探索的学生无论使用何种AI均能获得新想法，而长时间机械校对的学生即使使用苏格拉底式AI也难以产生创意。
AI.conclusion: AI写作工具设计应注重促进深度互动，而非单纯优化AI功能，以提升学生的创意生成能力。

authors_text: Jennie J. Y. Chen,Sidney S. Fels
title: Curves Ahead: Enhancing the Steering Law for Complex Curved Trajectories
title_zh: 前方曲线：增强复杂曲线轨迹的转向定律
abstract: The Steering Law has long been a fundamental model in predicting movement time for tasks involving navigating through constrained paths, such as in selecting sub-menu options, particularly for straight and circular arc trajectories. However, this does not reflect the complexities of real-world tasks where curvatures can vary arbitrarily, limiting its applications. This study aims to address this gap by introducing the total curvature parameter K into the equation to account for the overall curviness characteristic of a path. To validate this extension, we conducted a mouse-steering experiment on fixed-width paths with varying lengths and curviness levels. Our results demonstrate that the introduction of K significantly improves model fitness for movement time prediction over traditional models. These findings advance our understanding of movement in complex environments and support potential applications in fields like speech motor control and virtual navigation.
summary: The Steering Law has long been a fundamental model in predicting movement time for tasks involving navigating through constrained paths, such as in selecting sub-menu options, particularly for straight and circular arc trajectories. However, this does not reflect the complexities of real-world tasks where curvatures can vary arbitrarily, limiting its applications. This study aims to address this gap by introducing the total curvature parameter K into the equation to account for the overall curviness characteristic of a path. To validate this extension, we conducted a mouse-steering experiment on fixed-width paths with varying lengths and curviness levels. Our results demonstrate that the introduction of K significantly improves model fitness for movement time prediction over traditional models. These findings advance our understanding of movement in complex environments and support potential applications in fields like speech motor control and virtual navigation.
abstract_zh: 长期以来，转向定律一直是预测涉及通过受限路径导航的任务的运动时间的基本模型，例如选择子菜单选项，特别是对于直线和圆弧轨迹。然而，这并不能反映现实世界任务的复杂性，其中曲率可以任意变化，限制了其应用。本研究旨在通过将总曲率参数 K 引入方程来解决这一差距，以考虑路径的整体曲率特性。为了验证这一扩展，我们在具有不同长度和弯曲程度的固定宽度路径上进行了鼠标转向实验。我们的结果表明，与传统模型相比，K 的引入显着提高了运动时间预测的模型适应性。这些发现增进了我们对复杂环境中运动的理解，并支持语音运动控制和虚拟导航等领域的潜在应用。

AI.tldr: 该论文通过引入总曲率参数K改进Steering Law模型，以提升对复杂曲线轨迹运动时间预测的准确性。
AI.motivation: 传统Steering Law模型仅适用于直线和圆弧轨迹，无法反映现实任务中曲率任意变化的复杂路径特性，限制了其应用范围。
AI.method: 设计鼠标操控实验，在固定宽度路径上测试不同长度和曲率水平的任务，将总曲率参数K纳入模型进行验证。
AI.result: 实验表明引入K参数后，新模型在运动时间预测的拟合度上显著优于传统模型。
AI.conclusion: 该研究深化了对复杂环境运动规律的理解，为语音运动控制和虚拟导航等领域的应用提供了理论支持。

method: 设计鼠标操控实验，在固定宽度路径上测试不同长度和曲率水平的任务，将总曲率参数K纳入模型进行验证。
result: 实验表明引入K参数后，新模型在运动时间预测的拟合度上显著优于传统模型。
conclusion: 该研究深化了对复杂环境运动规律的理解，为语音运动控制和虚拟导航等领域的应用提供了理论支持。
search_text: Curves Ahead: Enhancing the Steering Law for Complex Curved Trajectories 前方曲线：增强复杂曲线轨迹的转向定律 The Steering Law has long been a fundamental model in predicting movement time for tasks involving navigating through constrained paths, such as in selecting sub-menu options, particularly for straight and circular arc trajectories. However, this does not reflect the complexities of real-world tasks where curvatures can vary arbitrarily, limiting its applications. This study aims to address this gap by introducing the total curvature parameter K into the equation to account for the overall curviness characteristic of a path. To validate this extension, we conducted a mouse-steering experiment on fixed-width paths with varying lengths and curviness levels. Our results demonstrate that the introduction of K significantly improves model fitness for movement time prediction over traditional models. These findings advance our understanding of movement in complex environments and support potential applications in fields like speech motor control and virtual navigation. 长期以来，转向定律一直是预测涉及通过受限路径导航的任务的运动时间的基本模型，例如选择子菜单选项，特别是对于直线和圆弧轨迹。然而，这并不能反映现实世界任务的复杂性，其中曲率可以任意变化，限制了其应用。本研究旨在通过将总曲率参数 K 引入方程来解决这一差距，以考虑路径的整体曲率特性。为了验证这一扩展，我们在具有不同长度和弯曲程度的固定宽度路径上进行了鼠标转向实验。我们的结果表明，与传统模型相比，K 的引入显着提高了运动时间预测的模型适应性。这些发现增进了我们对复杂环境中运动的理解，并支持语音运动控制和虚拟导航等领域的潜在应用。 该论文通过引入总曲率参数K改进Steering Law模型，以提升对复杂曲线轨迹运动时间预测的准确性。 Jennie J. Y. Chen,Sidney S. Fels cs.HC

authors_text: Eduard Tulchinskii,Daria Voronkova,Ilya Trofimov,Evgeny Burnaev,Serguei Barannikov
title: RTD-Lite: Scalable Topological Analysis for Comparing Weighted Graphs in Learning Tasks
title_zh: RTD-Lite：用于比较学习任务中的加权图的可扩展拓扑分析
abstract: Topological methods for comparing weighted graphs are valuable in various learning tasks but often suffer from computational inefficiency on large datasets. We introduce RTD-Lite, a scalable algorithm that efficiently compares topological features, specifically connectivity or cluster structures at arbitrary scales, of two weighted graphs with one-to-one correspondence between vertices. Using minimal spanning trees in auxiliary graphs, RTD-Lite captures topological discrepancies with $O(n^2)$ time and memory complexity. This efficiency enables its application in tasks like dimensionality reduction and neural network training. Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that RTD-Lite effectively identifies topological differences while significantly reducing computation time compared to existing methods. Moreover, integrating RTD-Lite into neural network training as a loss function component enhances the preservation of topological structures in learned representations. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/ArGintum/RTD-Lite
summary: Topological methods for comparing weighted graphs are valuable in various learning tasks but often suffer from computational inefficiency on large datasets. We introduce RTD-Lite, a scalable algorithm that efficiently compares topological features, specifically connectivity or cluster structures at arbitrary scales, of two weighted graphs with one-to-one correspondence between vertices. Using minimal spanning trees in auxiliary graphs, RTD-Lite captures topological discrepancies with $O(n^2)$ time and memory complexity. This efficiency enables its application in tasks like dimensionality reduction and neural network training. Experiments on synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that RTD-Lite effectively identifies topological differences while significantly reducing computation time compared to existing methods. Moreover, integrating RTD-Lite into neural network training as a loss function component enhances the preservation of topological structures in learned representations. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/ArGintum/RTD-Lite
abstract_zh: 用于比较加权图的拓扑方法在各种学习任务中都很有价值，但通常会遇到大型数据集上计算效率低下的问题。我们引入了 RTD-Lite，这是一种可扩展的算法，可以有效地比较两个加权图的拓扑特征，特别是任意尺度的连通性或簇结构，并且顶点之间具有一一对应关系。在辅助图中使用最小生成树，RTD-Lite 以 $O(n^2)$ 时间和内存复杂度捕获拓扑差异。这种效率使其能够应用于降维和神经网络训练等任务。对合成数据集和真实数据集的实验表明，与现有方法相比，RTD-Lite 可以有效识别拓扑差异，同时显着减少计算时间。此外，将 RTD-Lite 作为损失函数组件集成到神经网络训练中可以增强学习表示中拓扑结构的保存。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/ArGintum/RTD-Lite 上公开获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为RTD-Lite的可扩展算法，用于在学习任务中高效比较加权图的拓扑特征。
AI.motivation: 现有的拓扑方法在处理大规模数据集时计算效率低下，难以满足实际应用需求。
AI.method: 通过辅助图的最小生成树捕捉拓扑差异，时间与内存复杂度为O(n²)，并将其集成到神经网络训练中作为损失函数组件。
AI.result: 实验表明，RTD-Lite在合成和真实数据集上均能有效识别拓扑差异，显著减少计算时间，并提升神经网络对拓扑结构的保持能力。
AI.conclusion: RTD-Lite为大规模加权图的拓扑分析提供了高效解决方案，代码已开源供研究使用。

authors_text: Ang Li
title: Revisiting FastMap: New Applications
title_zh: 重温 FastMap：新应用
abstract: FastMap was first introduced in the Data Mining community for generating Euclidean embeddings of complex objects. In this dissertation, we first present FastMap to generate Euclidean embeddings of graphs in near-linear time: The pairwise Euclidean distances approximate a desired graph-based distance function on the vertices. We then apply the graph version of FastMap to efficiently solve various graph-theoretic problems of significant interest in AI: including facility location, top-K centrality computations, community detection and block modeling, and graph convex hull computations. We also present a novel learning framework, called FastMapSVM, by combining FastMap and Support Vector Machines. We then apply FastMapSVM to predict the satisfiability of Constraint Satisfaction Problems and to classify seismograms in Earthquake Science.
summary: FastMap was first introduced in the Data Mining community for generating Euclidean embeddings of complex objects. In this dissertation, we first present FastMap to generate Euclidean embeddings of graphs in near-linear time: The pairwise Euclidean distances approximate a desired graph-based distance function on the vertices. We then apply the graph version of FastMap to efficiently solve various graph-theoretic problems of significant interest in AI: including facility location, top-K centrality computations, community detection and block modeling, and graph convex hull computations. We also present a novel learning framework, called FastMapSVM, by combining FastMap and Support Vector Machines. We then apply FastMapSVM to predict the satisfiability of Constraint Satisfaction Problems and to classify seismograms in Earthquake Science.
abstract_zh: FastMap 首次在数据挖掘社区中引入，用于生成复杂对象的欧几里德嵌入。在本论文中，我们首先提出 FastMap 在近线性时间内生成图的欧几里德嵌入：成对欧几里德距离近似于顶点上所需的基于图的距离函数。然后，我们应用 FastMap 的图版本来有效解决人工智能中重要的各种图论问题：包括设施定位、top-K 中心性计算、社区检测和块建模以及图凸包计算。我们还通过结合 FastMap 和支持向量机，提出了一种新颖的学习框架，称为 FastMapSVM。然后，我们应用 FastMapSVM 来预测约束满足问题的可满足性，并对地震科学中的地震图进行分类。

AI.tldr: 本文重新审视并扩展了FastMap方法，用于生成图的欧几里得嵌入，并将其应用于解决AI领域的多个图论问题，同时提出结合支持向量机的新型学习框架FastMapSVM。
AI.motivation: 传统图嵌入方法计算复杂度高，难以满足AI领域对高效图分析的需求，需探索更高效的嵌入方法及其在实际问题中的应用潜力。
AI.method: 提出基于FastMap的图嵌入算法，在近线性时间内生成近似欧几里得嵌入；结合支持向量机设计FastMapSVM框架，用于约束满足问题可满足性预测和地震图分类。
AI.result: 实现了图嵌入的高效计算，成功应用于设施选址、社区检测等图论问题，并在地震学分类任务中取得有效结果。
AI.conclusion: FastMap方法在图嵌入与AI问题求解中具有高效性与实用性，其与机器学习的结合拓展了传统算法的应用边界。
